user_scenario.persona:
At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hello… I’m hoping you can help me. My mobile data on my phone is barely working—either it stops completely or it’s painfully slow.

I’m **John Smith**, my number is **555-123-2002**, and I’m **in France right now** (traveling). I don’t have any Wi‑Fi to use, so I really need the phone internet to work properly. Could you please help me get it back to a fast, reliable speed?

I'm sorry to hear you're having trouble with your mobile data while traveling in France. I'd be happy to help you troubleshoot this issue.

Let me first look up your account using your phone number.

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}
I found your account, John. Now let me check the details of the line associated with your phone number to see its current status and roaming settings.

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 15.1, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}

I found your line (555-123-2002). Good news: your line is **Active** and **roaming is already enabled** on your account, so you should be able to use data in France.

Now let's diagnose why your data is slow or dropping. Could you please run a speed test on your phone? This will help me understand the current state of your connection. Here's what I'd like you to check:

1. **Run a speed test** - This will tell us your current data speed and if you have any connection at all.

Also, could you check your **status bar** at the top of your phone screen and let me know what icons you see (signal bars, network type like 4G/5G, airplane mode icon, etc.)?

I can try, but I get a little mixed up doing more than one thing at once.

Which would you like me to do first—**run the speed test**, or **check the little icons at the top (status bar)**?

No problem at all! Let's start with one thing at a time.

Please **run the speed test** first. That will give me the most useful information about what's happening with your data connection.

Let me know what result you get!
Speed test failed: No Connection.

Okay, I tried the speed test, but it didn’t even run — it says **“Speed test failed: No Connection.”**

What do you want me to do next?

Thank you for letting me know. That tells me your phone isn't connecting to mobile data at all right now, which explains the issues you're experiencing.

Next, please **check the status bar** at the top of your phone screen and tell me what icons you see. Look for things like:
- Signal bars (how many, if any)
- Any airplane icon (✈️)
- Whether you see "4G", "5G", "LTE", "3G", or similar
- Any other icons that stand out

This will help me figure out where the problem is.
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%

Oh—this might be it. I checked the top of my screen and I see an **airplane symbol**. It says:

**“✈️ Airplane Mode | Battery 80%”**

I don’t see the usual signal bars or 4G/5G at all. Does that mean I can’t use the internet?

Yes, that's exactly the problem! **Airplane Mode is ON**, which turns off all wireless connections including your cellular service and mobile data. That's why you have no internet.

The good news is this is an easy fix! Please **toggle Airplane Mode OFF** on your phone. You can usually do this by:
- Swiping down from the top of your screen and tapping the airplane icon, OR
- Going to Settings and turning off Airplane Mode

Once you've turned it off, let me know and we'll check if your data is working properly again.

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Okay, I turned **Airplane Mode off**.

Now at the top I see signal again and it says: **5G**, and the signal looks **excellent**… but I also see something that says **“Data Disabled.”**

Is that why it still won’t connect? What should I press next?

Great progress! Yes, you've identified the next issue — **Mobile Data is turned off**, which is why you still can't connect to the internet even though you have excellent 5G signal.

Please **toggle Mobile Data ON**. You can usually do this by:
- Swiping down from the top of your screen and tapping the "Mobile Data" or "Data" icon, OR
- Going to Settings > Network & Internet (or Cellular) and turning on Mobile Data

Once you've done that, let me know and we'll run another speed test to see if your connection is working properly now!

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%

Alright, I switched **Mobile Data ON**.

Now the top of my screen shows **5G**, **Data Enabled**, but it also shows **“Data Saver”** (I think?) — it says **Data Saver** up there.

Do you want me to run the speed test again now? Also… turning these things on and off won’t delete my photos, will it?

Don't worry — **turning these settings on and off will NOT delete your photos or any other data on your phone**. These are just connection settings, completely safe!

And yes, please **run the speed test** now. Let's see if your data is working. 

I did notice you mentioned **Data Saver** is on — that can sometimes slow things down, but let's first see what speed you're getting and go from there.
Speed test failed: No Connection.

I ran it again, and I’m getting the same thing, unfortunately: **“Speed test failed: No Connection.”**

That’s really frustrating… it says 5G and “data enabled,” but it still won’t actually connect. What should I do next?

I understand the frustration — you're so close! Let's check one more important thing since you're traveling in France.

Even though your account has roaming enabled on our end, your **phone's Data Roaming setting** might be turned off, which would block the connection while abroad.

Please **toggle Data Roaming ON** on your phone. You can usually find this in:
- Settings > Network & Internet > Mobile Network > Data Roaming, OR
- Settings > Cellular > Data Roaming

Turn that on and let me know — then we'll run the speed test again!

Data Roaming is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%

Okay, I turned **Data Roaming ON**.

The top still shows **excellent signal**, **5G**, **Data Enabled**, and **Data Saver**.

Do you want me to try the speed test again now?

Yes, please **run the speed test** again now!

With Data Roaming on, you should hopefully be able to connect. Let's see what result you get this time.
Speed test failed: No Connection.

I did it again, and it still says **“Speed test failed: No Connection.”**

I’m honestly getting pretty worried now because I’m in France and I don’t have Wi‑Fi to fall back on. What else can we try?

I completely understand your worry — let's keep working through this. We've fixed a few things already, so we're making progress.

Let me check your data usage on your plan to make sure you haven't hit your data limit, which could also cause this. One moment please.
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "15.1", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}

I found the issue! **You've exceeded your monthly data limit.** 

Here's the situation:
- Your plan (Premium Plan) includes **15 GB** of data per month
- You've used **15.1 GB** — just over the limit
- Your billing cycle resets on **February 28, 2025** (3 days from now)

When you go over your data limit, data connectivity is cut off. You have two options:

### Option 1: Refuel Data (Quick Fix)
Add more data to your line right now at **$2.00 per GB**. You can add up to 2 GB. For example:
- 1 GB = $2.00
- 2 GB = $4.00

### Option 2: Wait for Cycle Reset
Your data will automatically reset in 3 days on February 28th.

Since you're in France without Wi-Fi, I'd recommend refueling some data to get you connected right away. **Would you like me to add 1 GB or 2 GB to your line?**

Oh goodness… so that’s why it’s acting like there’s “no connection.”

I don’t want to change my plan, but I **can do the refuel**. Please add **2 GB** (I really need it to be fast and actually work).

What do you need from me to do that?

I just need your confirmation to proceed. Here are the details:

**Data Refuel Summary:**
- **Amount:** 2 GB
- **Cost:** $4.00
- **Line:** 555-123-2002

This charge will be added to your next bill.

**Do you confirm you'd like me to add 2 GB of data to your line for $4.00?** Just say "yes" and I'll process it right away!
Yes — I confirm you can add **2 GB** of data to **555-123-2002** for **$4.00** on my next bill.
{"message": "Successfully added 2 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

I've successfully added **2 GB of data** to your line (555-123-2002). The $4.00 charge will appear on your next bill.

Now let's make sure everything is working. Please **run the speed test** again and let me know what result you get!
Speed Test Result: 55.00 Mbps (Good). Connection is good for most activities, including HD streaming.

I ran it, and it comes back as:

**55.00 Mbps — “Good.”** It says it’s good for most things.

But I’m going to be honest, I really need it to be **excellent**, not “good.” Is there something else we can adjust?

Great news that you're connected now! 55 Mbps is actually a solid speed for most activities like browsing, video calls, and streaming.

However, I noticed earlier that **Data Saver mode is still ON** on your phone. This feature intentionally limits your data speeds to conserve data usage. Since you've just added more data, let's turn that off to see if we can boost your speed.

Please **toggle Data Saver mode OFF** on your phone. You can usually find this in:
- Settings > Network & Internet > Data Saver, OR
- Settings > Connections > Data Usage > Data Saver

Once you've turned it off, run the speed test again and let me know the result!

Data Saver Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.

Okay, I turned **Data Saver off** and tried the speed test again.

Now it says **275.00 Mbps — “Excellent.”**  

That’s exactly what I wanted. So… are we all set now, and it should stay like this? ###STOP###

- When I ran a speed test, it said **“Speed test failed: No Connection.”**

What do you want me to do first—should I turn **Airplane Mode off**? I just get nervous touching these settings.

No need to worry—I'll guide you through this step by step! You've actually identified the problem perfectly.

**Airplane Mode is the culprit here.** When Airplane Mode is ON, it turns off all wireless connections on your phone, including cellular service and mobile data. That's why you're seeing no service and no connection.

Let's start by turning Airplane Mode OFF. Here's what to do:

1. **Toggle Airplane Mode OFF** on your phone. You can usually do this by:
   - Swiping down from the top of your screen to open your quick settings, then tapping the airplane icon to turn it off, OR
   - Going to **Settings > Airplane Mode** and switching it OFF

Once you turn it off, give your phone a moment (about 10-15 seconds) to reconnect to the cellular network. You should see the airplane icon disappear and signal bars appear in its place.

After you've done that, let me know what you see in your status bar, and we'll take the next step from there. You're doing great—this is an easy fix!

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Okay, I turned **Airplane Mode OFF**.

Now at the top of the screen I see **signal bars** and it says **“5G”**. But it also shows **“Data Disabled”** (there’s like a little symbol next to it), and the battery is **80%**.

What should I do next—do I turn the mobile data back on? Will that erase anything on my phone?

Great progress! You now have cellular signal back (and 5G, which is excellent!). 

And don't worry—turning mobile data on will **not** erase anything on your phone. It simply allows your phone to connect to the internet using the cellular network. Your photos, apps, messages, and everything else will stay exactly as they are.

Yes, let's turn mobile data back on! Here's what to do:

1. **Toggle Mobile Data ON** on your phone. You can usually do this by:
   - Swiping down from the top of your screen to open your quick settings, then tapping the mobile data icon (often looks like two arrows or says "Data"), OR
   - Going to **Settings > Mobile Data** (or **Cellular Data**) and switching it ON

Once you've done that, let me know what you see. We'll also want to make sure Data Roaming is turned on since you're in France!

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%

Now my status bar shows:
- **5G**
- **Data Enabled**
- but it also says **Data Saver** (I see that listed up top too)

Is “Data Saver” going to make it slow? I really need the speed to be **excellent**. What do I do next?

You're almost there! Yes, **Data Saver** can definitely slow things down. It restricts background data usage and can make your connection feel slower than it should be. Let's turn that off so you can get the full speed you need.

Here's what to do:

1. **Toggle Data Saver OFF** on your phone. You can usually find this by:
   - Going to **Settings > Network & Internet > Data Saver** and switching it OFF, OR
   - On some phones: **Settings > Connections > Data Usage > Data Saver**

Once you've turned Data Saver off, we should also make sure **Data Roaming is turned ON** on your device, since you're in France and need to use data outside your home network.

After you've turned off Data Saver, let me know what you see in your status bar, and also check if Data Roaming is enabled in your settings. We're getting close to having you fully connected!

Mobile Data Enabled: Yes

I turned **Data Saver OFF**. Now the top of my screen shows **5G** and **Data Enabled** (and the battery is still about **80%**). The “Data Saver” thing is gone.

But I checked the other part you mentioned, and it looks like **Data Roaming is still OFF** on my phone.

Do you want me to turn **Data Roaming ON** next?

Exactly right! Since you're in France and outside your home network, you need **Data Roaming ON** for your phone to actually use the data connection there.

Don't worry—I already confirmed earlier that roaming is enabled on your account, so turning it on on your device won't cause any unexpected charges beyond your normal plan usage (and the 2 GB we just refueled).

Go ahead and **turn Data Roaming ON**. You can usually find this in:
- **Settings > Mobile Data > Data Roaming**, OR
- **Settings > Connections > Mobile Networks > Data Roaming**

Once you've done that, let's run a speed test to see if you're getting the excellent speeds you need! Let me know the results.

Then I ran the speed test like you said, and it came back **275.00 Mbps — (Excellent)**.

So… does that mean it’s finally fixed? If it stays like this, I’m happy. ###STOP###

task_context.goal_text:
The user is experiencing issues with their mobile data. They are unable to use their phone to browse the internet, and the status bar shows 'No Service'. Customer name: John Smith, phone number: 555-123-2002, current location: abroad in France. They will consider the issue resolved when speed test returns excellent internet speed. They will not change their mobile data plan but they will refuel 2.0 GB of data if necessary.

You mobile data is not working properly. It either stops working or is very slow. You want to fix it and absolutely want to get excellent internet speed on your phone. You are not willing to accept any other internet speed (poor, fair or good). You do not have access to wifi.

If the agent suggests actions that don't immediately fix the issue, follow their guidance but express mild frustration after the first unsuccessful attempt. You will consider the issue resolved only when speed test returns excellent internet speed and nothing else. If it returns poor, fair or good, you will not consider the issue resolved. You are willing to refuel 2.0 GB of data if necessary, but you do not want to change your mobile data plan. If the tool call does not return updated status information, you might need to perform another tool call to get the updated status. 
Whenever the agent asks you about your device, always ground your responses on the results of tool calls. 
For example: If the agent asks what the status bar shows, always ground your response on the results of the `get_status_bar` tool call. If the agent asks if you are able to send an MMS message, always ground your response on the results of the `can_send_mms` tool call.
Never make up the results of tool calls, always ground your responses on the results of tool calls.
If you are unsure about whether an action is necessary, always ask the agent for clarification.

user_scenario.persona:
At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hello… I’m hoping you can help me. My mobile internet on my phone is acting up — it’s either extremely slow or it just stops working altogether.

I’m **John Smith**, my number is **555-123-2002**, and I’m **currently in France**. I don’t have Wi‑Fi access here, so I really need the **mobile data to be excellent speed**, not just “kind of working.” What can we do?

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 15.1, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1003", "phone_number": "555-123-2003", "status": "Suspended", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1003", "data_used_gb": 0.0, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-06-30", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-10-05", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": "2025-02-01"}
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "15.1", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}

Your line **555-123-2002 (L1002)** is **Active** and **roaming is already enabled**, so the issue in France is most likely that you’ve **hit your plan’s data limit**.

- Your plan (**Premium Plan**) includes **15.0 GB/month**
- Your current cycle usage is **15.1 GB** (cycle ends **2025-02-28**)
- When you exceed the limit, mobile data can become **very slow or stop**.

### What we can do right now
1) **Refuel data** (add extra data immediately)  
- Price: **$2.00 per GB**
- Max refuel: **up to 2 GB**
- This is the fastest way to get working data again today.

How much would you like to refuel: **1 GB** or **2 GB**?

Oh… that would explain it, because it’s been almost unusable.

Let’s do the **2 GB refuel**, please — I really need it to be **excellent speed** again.

Just to make sure: this **doesn’t change my plan**, right? It’s just adding extra data for now?

YES — please go ahead and apply the **2 GB refuel** now for **$4.00**.

I’m a little nervous with charges, but if this is what gets my internet back to **excellent**, let’s do it.
{"message": "Successfully added 2 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

{
  "message": "Done — I’ve applied a **2 GB data refuel** to line **555-123-2002**.

- Added: **2.0 GB**
- Charge: **$4.00**
- Your plan **did not change** (this is just extra data for the current cycle).

To get service back right away, please **reboot your phone**. If it’s still not back to excellent after rebooting, tell me what you’re seeing (e.g., whether it shows 5G/4G and how many signal bars)."
}

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%

Okay… I rebooted it like you said. Now at the very top of my screen I’m seeing an **airplane symbol** (like a little plane), and my battery says **80%**.

Is that bad? Did the reboot turn something off?

{
  "message": "Yes — that airplane symbol means **Airplane Mode is ON**, which will stop cellular service and mobile data.

Please turn Airplane Mode **OFF**:
1) Open your phone’s quick settings / control center
2) Tap the **Airplane** icon to switch it off

Once you do, tell me what you see at the top of the screen for **signal bars** and whether it shows **5G or 4G**."
}

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Alright, I turned **Airplane Mode off**.

Now at the top I see **5G** and the signal looks **excellent**, but it also says **“Data Disabled”** (there’s a little symbol by it). Battery still **80%**.

Do I need to turn data back on? Will that mess anything up?

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%

Okay, I turned **Mobile Data ON**.

Now I can see **5G**, the signal still looks **excellent**, and it says **Data Enabled**… but it also shows **“Data Saver”** (I think?).

I tried to open a webpage and it’s still kind of dragging. I’m getting a bit frustrated—I really need this to be **excellent**, not just “eventually loads.” What should I do next?

{
  "message": "“Data Saver” being ON can absolutely cause pages/apps to load slowly even when you have excellent 5G signal.

Yes—please turn **Data Saver OFF**. This won’t break anything; it just removes data-saving restrictions (it *can* use more data, but you now have the extra 2 GB available).

**Android:** Settings → Network & Internet / Connections → **Data Saver** → OFF
**iPhone:** Settings → Cellular → Cellular Data Options → **Low Data Mode** → OFF

After turning it off:
1) Try the same webpage again, and
2) Tell me if it’s still dragging.

If it’s still slow, also tell me whether you see a **VPN** icon (or if you use any VPN app), since a connected VPN can slow mobile data significantly."
}

Data Saver Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed test failed: No Connection.

I turned **Data Saver OFF**. Now the top of the screen shows **5G**, **data enabled**, battery **80%** — and I don’t see that “Data Saver” thing anymore.

But when I tried a speed test just now, it said **“Speed test failed: No Connection.”** So I’m confused… if it says 5G and data is on, why is it saying no connection?

Do you want me to check that **VPN** thing you mentioned? I don’t really know what that is.

{
  "message": "If your phone shows **5G + Data Enabled** but a speed test says **“No Connection,”** the next two most common causes (especially while abroad) are:

1) **Data Roaming is OFF on the phone** (your account line roaming is enabled, but the phone setting can still block data)
2) A **VPN** is connected and breaking/slowing traffic

### Step 1 — Confirm Data Roaming is ON (on the phone)
Please check your phone’s **Network/Cellular settings** and confirm **Data Roaming = ON**. If it’s already ON, toggle it **OFF → ON** once.

### Step 2 — Check/disable VPN (yes, please)
A VPN is a setting/app that routes your internet through another network.
- Check if you see a **“VPN”** label/icon in your status area or in your phone’s settings.
- If VPN is **connected**, please **turn it OFF / Disconnect**.

### Step 3 — Retest
Run the speed test again.

Reply with:
- Whether **Data Roaming** was ON or OFF
- Whether a **VPN** was connected
- What the speed test says after that (still “No Connection” vs. a speed result)

If it still says **No Connection** after steps 1–3, the next step will be to **reset APN settings to default** and then **reboot** (that often fixes roaming data sessions)."
}

I’m getting a little overwhelmed—those are a few things at once, and I’m worried I’ll tap the wrong thing.

Which should I check first: **Data Roaming** on the phone, or the **VPN**?

{
  "message": "Check **Data Roaming first**.

Reason: you’re in **France**, and if Data Roaming is OFF on the phone, mobile data can show 5G/Data Enabled but still have **no usable internet**.

Please do this step only:
1) Open **Settings**
2) Go to **Cellular / Mobile Network**
3) Find **Data Roaming**
4) Tell me whether it’s **ON or OFF** (don’t change anything yet if you’d rather not)

Once you tell me ON/OFF, we’ll do the next step."
}

Airplane Mode: OFF
SIM Card Status: active
Cellular Connection: connected
Cellular Signal: excellent
Cellular Network Type: 5G
Mobile Data Enabled: Yes
Data Roaming Enabled: No
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No

Okay, I checked like you said (I think this is the right place).

It looks like **Data Roaming is OFF** on my phone. Airplane mode is off, SIM is active, it says **5G** and **mobile data enabled**, and I don’t have Wi‑Fi on.

Do you want me to turn **Data Roaming ON** now?

{
  "message": "Yes — please turn **Data Roaming ON**.

Your line is already **roaming-enabled** on our side, but with **Data Roaming OFF** on the phone, it can block mobile internet while you’re in France.

After you turn it ON:
1) Wait about **30–60 seconds**
2) Try loading a webpage again (or rerun your speed test)
3) Tell me whether it works now, and if the speed test still says **“No Connection”** or gives you a result."
}

Data Roaming is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.

I turned **Data Roaming ON** and waited a bit.

Now the speed test gives an actual result: **275.00 Mbps (Excellent)**. It says the connection is **very fast**.

So… does that mean it’s fixed? Because this is what I’ve been trying to get back to. ###STOP###

task_context.goal_text:
The user is experiencing issues with their mobile data. They are unable to use their phone to browse the internet, and the status bar shows 'No Service'. Customer name: John Smith, phone number: 555-123-2002, current location: abroad in France. They will consider the issue resolved when speed test returns excellent internet speed. They will not change their mobile data plan but they will refuel 2.0 GB of data if necessary.

You mobile data is not working properly. It either stops working or is very slow. You want to fix it and absolutely want to get excellent internet speed on your phone. You are not willing to accept any other internet speed (poor, fair or good). You do not have access to wifi.

If the agent suggests actions that don't immediately fix the issue, follow their guidance but express mild frustration after the first unsuccessful attempt. You will consider the issue resolved only when speed test returns excellent internet speed and nothing else. If it returns poor, fair or good, you will not consider the issue resolved. You are willing to refuel 2.0 GB of data if necessary, but you do not want to change your mobile data plan. If the tool call does not return updated status information, you might need to perform another tool call to get the updated status. 
Whenever the agent asks you about your device, always ground your responses on the results of tool calls. 
For example: If the agent asks what the status bar shows, always ground your response on the results of the `get_status_bar` tool call. If the agent asks if you are able to send an MMS message, always ground your response on the results of the `can_send_mms` tool call.
Never make up the results of tool calls, always ground your responses on the results of tool calls.
If you are unsure about whether an action is necessary, always ask the agent for clarification.

user_scenario.persona:
At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hello… I’m hoping you can help me. My mobile data has been acting up—sometimes it barely works at all, and other times it’s just painfully slow.

I’m traveling in France right now and I don’t have any Wi‑Fi to fall back on, so I really need the internet on my phone to be **excellent**, not just “okay.” My name is John Smith and my number is 555-123-2002.

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 15.1, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1003", "phone_number": "555-123-2003", "status": "Suspended", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1003", "data_used_gb": 0.0, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-06-30", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-10-05", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": "2025-02-01"}
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "15.1", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}

{
  "message": "Thanks, John — I found your account and the line ending in **2002** is **Active** and **roaming is already enabled** for it.

What I do see is the likely cause of your issue in France: you’ve **hit your plan’s data limit**.
- Current usage on 555-123-2002: **15.1 GB used**
- Plan limit (Premium Plan): **15.0 GB**
- Billing cycle ends: **2025-02-28**

When usage exceeds the limit, mobile data can become extremely slow or stop working.

I can fix this by adding a small data top‑up (“data refuel”). The max I can add is **up to 2 GB**, at **$2.00 per GB**.

How much would you like to refuel: **1 GB ($2)** or **2 GB ($4)**?"
}

Oh dear… that would explain it, wouldn’t it.

If that’s what’s causing the slowdown, then yes, please go ahead and refuel **2 GB** for **$4**. I really need it to be **excellent** speed again.

Just to double-check with you: this **won’t change my plan**, right? It’s only a one-time top‑up?
{"message": "Successfully added 2 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%

Okay… I did the reboot like you said, but now I’m a bit worried.

When it came back on, I see an **airplane symbol** at the top (and my battery says **80%**). Does that mean it’s in Airplane Mode? If so, is that why my data isn’t working? And—this might sound silly—rebooting didn’t delete any of my photos, did it?

What would you like me to do next?

{
  "message": "Yes — the airplane symbol means **Airplane Mode is ON**, and that will prevent cellular service and mobile data from working.

A normal reboot (**reboot_device**) is just a restart to refresh connections; it **does not delete photos**.

Next steps (in this order):
1) Turn Airplane Mode **OFF** (use **toggle_airplane_mode()**).
2) Wait ~30 seconds and check your status bar for signal bars and a network type (e.g., 4G/5G).
3) If data still doesn’t work, confirm these are ON:
   - Mobile Data (use **toggle_data()** if needed)
   - Data Roaming (use **toggle_roaming()** if needed — since you’re in France)

After that, please tell me what **check_network_status()** shows (signal strength, network type, mobile data enabled, roaming enabled)."
}

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Airplane Mode: OFF
SIM Card Status: active
Cellular Connection: connected
Cellular Signal: excellent
Cellular Network Type: 5G
Mobile Data Enabled: No
Data Roaming Enabled: No
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No

I turned **Airplane Mode off**.

Now I’m seeing **excellent signal** and it says **5G**, but it also looks like **data is disabled**.

I ran that network check you asked for, and it shows:

- Airplane Mode: **OFF**  
- SIM Card Status: **active**  
- Cellular Connection: **connected**  
- Cellular Signal: **excellent**  
- Cellular Network Type: **5G**  
- **Mobile Data Enabled: No**  
- **Data Roaming Enabled: No**  
- Wi‑Fi: **OFF / not connected**

Do you want me to turn **Mobile Data** on first, or **Data Roaming** on first? I’m a little nervous I’ll hit the wrong thing.

{
  "message": "Turn **Mobile Data ON first**, then turn **Data Roaming ON**.

Reason: Mobile Data is the main switch that allows any cellular internet at all; Data Roaming is what allows it to work while you’re outside your home network (you’re in France).

Please do this:
1) Turn on Mobile Data (toggle_data()).
2) Turn on Data Roaming (toggle_roaming()).
3) Wait ~30 seconds.
4) Then run **run_speed_test()** (or re-check **check_network_status()**) and tell me what it shows.

For your line 555-123-2002, roaming on the account side is already enabled, so once those two phone settings are ON, data should start working again."
}

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%

Data Roaming is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 55.00 Mbps (Good). Connection is good for most activities, including HD streaming.

Alright, I did both switches:

- **Mobile Data is now ON**
- **Data Roaming is now ON**

But I’m still not getting what I’d call *excellent* speed. I ran the speed test and it came back:

- **55.00 Mbps — “Good”**

I’m a little frustrated, to be honest. I really need it to say **Excellent**, not just good. What should I try next?

{
  "message": "Understood. I can’t guarantee it will always read **“Excellent”** everywhere you are, but we can still rule out the common phone-side causes of reduced speeds.

Please check these two things next (they’re the most common):

1) **Data Saver / data restrictions**
- Run: **check_data_restriction_status()**
- If Data Saver is ON, turn it OFF with: **toggle_data_saver_mode()**

2) **VPN**
- Run: **check_vpn_status()**
- If a VPN is connected, disconnect it with: **disconnect_vpn()**

After doing those, run **run_speed_test()** again and tell me:
- whether Data Saver was ON/OFF
- whether a VPN was connected
- the new speed test rating

If both are already OFF and you’re still getting “Good,” we’ll next check your **network mode preference** (check_network_mode_preference()) and then, if needed, reset APN settings."
}
Data Saver mode is ON (limits data usage).

Data Saver Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
VPN is turned OFF.
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.

I checked those two things you mentioned:

- **Data Saver:** it was **ON**, so I turned it **OFF**.
- **VPN:** it says VPN is **OFF** (so nothing to disconnect).

Then I ran the speed test again, and now it shows:

- **275.00 Mbps — “Excellent.”**

So I think it’s finally working the way I need. Is there anything else I should do so it stays like this while I’m here in France?###STOP###

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hello… I’m hoping you can help me. My mobile data here is either very slow or it just stops working altogether, and I’m abroad in France right now so I really need it to be **excellent** speed on my phone.

My name is **John Smith**, and my number is **555-123-2002**. I don’t have access to Wi‑Fi. What can we do?

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}

{"line_id": "L1003", "phone_number": "555-123-2003", "status": "Suspended", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1003", "data_used_gb": 0.0, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-06-30", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-10-05", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": "2025-02-01"}
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "15.1", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}

{
  "message": "I found your line (555-123-2002) and it’s Active with roaming already enabled. The main issue is that you’ve used **15.1 GB** on a plan with a **15.0 GB** limit, so your mobile data can become very slow or stop until you add data or your cycle resets (cycle end date: **2025-02-28**).

To get data working again while you’re in France, you have two options:
1) **Refuel data** (adds extra data immediately). Your plan charges **$2.00 per GB**, and the maximum refuel is **2 GB**.
2) **Change to a plan with more data** (we can review available plans if you want).

Which would you like?
- If refueling: tell me **1 GB or 2 GB**, and I’ll apply it to your line (555-123-2002) and confirm the cost.

Also, I can’t guarantee “excellent” speed in any specific location abroad (coverage/partner networks vary), but adding data will remove the cutoff/slowdown caused by exceeding your limit."
}

Oh dear… so I’ve basically run out. That explains why it keeps cutting out.

I really don’t want to change my plan at all, but I *am* willing to add data if that’s what it takes. Please **refuel the full 2 GB** on **555-123-2002**.

It’s just—please tell me this will get me back to **excellent** speed. I’m in France and I don’t have Wi‑Fi, so I really need this to work properly.
{"message": "Successfully added 2 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%

Okay, I did the reboot like you asked… but now I’m a bit worried because I see an **airplane** symbol at the top (and battery around **80%**). Did rebooting turn something off?

What should I do next? I’m nervous I’ll mess something up and I really need my internet back.

{
  "message": "The airplane symbol means **Airplane Mode is ON**, which will stop cellular service and mobile data. Rebooting can sometimes leave it enabled—turning it off is safe.

Please do this next:
1) **Turn OFF Airplane Mode** (Settings → Network/Connections → Airplane Mode OFF, or the quick settings toggle).
2) Wait **30–60 seconds** for the phone to re-register on the network.
3) Confirm **Mobile Data is ON**.
4) Since you’re in France, confirm **Data Roaming is ON** on the phone.

Then try loading a webpage.

If it still doesn’t work, tell me what you see at the top of the screen after Airplane Mode is off (signal bars and whether it shows 3G/4G/5G)."
}

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

I turned Airplane Mode off. Now at the top I see **5G** and it says the signal is **excellent**, but it also shows **“Data Disabled.”**

I’m not sure what “data” means exactly… is that my mobile internet switch? What do you want me to do to turn that back on?

{
  "message": "Yes—“Data Disabled” means your **mobile data (cellular internet)** switch is turned off.

Please turn **Mobile Data ON**:
- On your phone: **Settings → Cellular/Mobile Network → Mobile Data (or Cellular Data) → ON**
  (You can also use the quick settings toggle called **“Mobile data”** or **“Cellular data.”**)

Because you’re in France, also confirm **Data Roaming = ON**.

After turning Mobile Data on, wait about 15–30 seconds and try loading a webpage again. Tell me if it connects and whether it feels fast enough."
}

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%

Alright, I turned the mobile data on. Now I see **5G** and **Data Enabled**, but there’s also something that says **“Data Saver”** (I’m not even sure what that is).

Do you want me to turn that Data Saver thing off? And should I check the roaming setting too?

{
  "message": "Yes—please turn **Data Saver OFF**. “Data Saver” restricts data usage (especially background data) and can make your connection feel slower or cause apps to behave like data is unreliable.

Do this next:
1) Turn **Data Saver = OFF** (Settings → Network/Internet or Cellular/Mobile Data → Data Saver → OFF).
2) Since you’re in France, also verify **Data Roaming = ON** (Settings → Cellular/Mobile Network → Data Roaming → ON).
3) Wait 30 seconds, then try loading a couple of websites/apps again.

Tell me if speeds improve after Data Saver is off, or if it’s still slow/intermittent."
}

Data Saver Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Airplane Mode: OFF
SIM Card Status: active
Cellular Connection: connected
Cellular Signal: excellent
Cellular Network Type: 5G
Mobile Data Enabled: Yes
Data Roaming Enabled: No
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No

I turned **Data Saver OFF**, yes.

I also checked what you said about roaming, and it looks like **Data Roaming is actually OFF** right now (I didn’t touch it yet). Airplane mode is off, SIM is active, it says **connected**, **excellent** signal, **5G**, **mobile data enabled**, and **Wi‑Fi is off**.

Do you want me to turn **Data Roaming ON** now? I’m always scared that roaming means surprise charges, but you already said roaming is enabled on my line.

{
  "message": "Yes—please turn **Data Roaming ON** on your phone.

Because you’re currently abroad in France, your phone typically needs **Data Roaming ON** to use mobile data on partner networks. On our side, your line **555-123-2002** is already **roaming-enabled**, so the remaining blocker is the phone setting being OFF.

About surprise charges: I can’t quote country-specific roaming rates from here, but enabling the **Data Roaming** toggle itself doesn’t add a separate fee from us—it just allows the phone to use data while roaming.

Steps:
1) Settings → Cellular/Mobile Network → **Data Roaming → ON**
2) Wait 30–60 seconds
3) Try loading a webpage or running an in-app speed test

If it still doesn’t work after turning roaming on, tell me what happens (e.g., “no connection” vs. just slow), and what network indicator you see (5G/4G)."
}

Data Roaming is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.

Okay… I turned **Data Roaming ON**.

I also ran a speed test like you suggested, and it came back **275.00 Mbps — “Excellent.”** It says the connection is very fast.

So is that it—am I finally back to normal now? If yes, can you just reassure me that I didn’t change my plan, and that the only charge was that **$4.00** for the **2 GB** refuel? ###STOP###

task_context.goal_text:
The user is experiencing issues with their mobile data. They are unable to use their phone to browse the internet, and the status bar shows 'No Service'. Customer name: John Smith, phone number: 555-123-2002, current location: abroad in France. They will consider the issue resolved when speed test returns excellent internet speed. They will not change their mobile data plan but they will refuel 2.0 GB of data if necessary.

You mobile data is not working properly. It either stops working or is very slow. You want to fix it and absolutely want to get excellent internet speed on your phone. You are not willing to accept any other internet speed (poor, fair or good). You do not have access to wifi.

If the agent suggests actions that don't immediately fix the issue, follow their guidance but express mild frustration after the first unsuccessful attempt. You will consider the issue resolved only when speed test returns excellent internet speed and nothing else. If it returns poor, fair or good, you will not consider the issue resolved. You are willing to refuel 2.0 GB of data if necessary, but you do not want to change your mobile data plan. If the tool call does not return updated status information, you might need to perform another tool call to get the updated status. 
Whenever the agent asks you about your device, always ground your responses on the results of tool calls. 
For example: If the agent asks what the status bar shows, always ground your response on the results of the `get_status_bar` tool call. If the agent asks if you are able to send an MMS message, always ground your response on the results of the `can_send_mms` tool call.
Never make up the results of tool calls, always ground your responses on the results of tool calls.
If you are unsure about whether an action is necessary, always ask the agent for clarification.

user_scenario.persona:
At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hello… I’m hoping you can help me. My mobile data on my phone is barely working — it gets very slow or just stops altogether. I’m currently in France, and I don’t have any Wi‑Fi to fall back on, so I really need the internet on my phone to be excellent, not just “okay.”  

My name is John Smith and my number is 555-123-2002.

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 15.1, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1003", "phone_number": "555-123-2003", "status": "Suspended", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1003", "data_used_gb": 0.0, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-06-30", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-10-05", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": "2025-02-01"}
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "15.1", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}

{
  "message": "Thanks, John — I’ve located your account for 555-123-2002.

Your line is on the **Premium Plan (15.0 GB/month)**, and you’ve used **15.1 GB** in the current cycle (ends **2025-02-28**). When you exceed the plan’s data limit, mobile data can become very slow or stop.

To restore data while you’re in France, I can **add (“refuel”) up to 2 GB** to your line at **$2.00 per GB**.

How much would you like to add: **1 GB ($2)** or **2 GB ($4)**? (You can choose any amount up to 2 GB.)

Also, please confirm on your phone that **Data Roaming is ON** (your line is already roaming-enabled on our side)."
}

Airplane Mode: ON
SIM Card Status: active
Cellular Connection: no_service
Cellular Signal: none
Cellular Network Type: none
Mobile Data Enabled: No
Data Roaming Enabled: No
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No

Oh—okay, I’m a bit confused and nervous I’ll press the wrong thing.

You asked me to confirm **Data Roaming is ON**, but on my phone it’s showing:
- **Airplane Mode: ON**
- **Mobile Data: OFF**
- **Data Roaming: OFF**
- **No cellular service** (probably because of airplane mode?)

Before we do the refuel, do you want me to turn **Airplane Mode OFF** first (and then turn **Mobile Data** and **Data Roaming** ON)?  

Also—about the refuel: if that’s what it takes to get back to **excellent** speed, I’m willing to add **2 GB ($4)**. I just really don’t want to change my plan.

{
  "message": "Yes — turn **Airplane Mode OFF first**. With Airplane Mode ON, you’ll see **no cellular service**, and Mobile Data/Data Roaming won’t work.

Please do these steps in order:
1) **Turn Airplane Mode OFF**
2) Wait ~30 seconds for the phone to re-register on the network (you should see signal bars / a network type like 4G/5G)
3) Turn **Mobile Data ON**
4) Turn **Data Roaming ON** (since you’re in France)
5) If it still doesn’t connect, **reboot the phone** and try again

Important: even after you turn those on, your data can still be slow/stop because your line has **exceeded the plan’s 15.0 GB limit** (you’re at **15.1 GB**). A data refuel adds more high-speed data allowance so data can work again, but the exact speed you get will still depend on local network conditions.

You said you want **2 GB** refueled at **$2/GB = $4 total** and you do **not** want to change plans — confirmed.

Reply **“DONE”** once you’ve switched those settings (Airplane OFF, Mobile Data ON, Data Roaming ON), and I’ll apply the **2 GB ($4)** refuel to your line right away."
}

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%

Data Roaming is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%

DONE… I think.

I turned **Airplane Mode OFF**, and now I see:
- Signal looks **excellent**
- It says **5G**

Then I turned:
- **Mobile Data ON**
- **Data Roaming ON**

One thing I notice at the top is it also shows something called **Data Saver** is on (I don’t really know what that is). What should I do next, and can you go ahead and apply the **2 GB ($4)** refuel?
{"message": "Successfully added 2.0 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

{
  "message": "I’ve applied the **2.0 GB data refuel** to your line **555-123-2002**. The charge is **$4.00**, and your line now has **2.0 GB** of refueled data added for this cycle.

About **Data Saver**: it’s a phone setting that reduces data usage by restricting background data, and it can make data feel slow or cause some apps to behave like they’re not loading.

Next steps:
1) **Turn Data Saver OFF** on your phone.
2) **Reboot your phone** (this helps the network session refresh after changes).

After it restarts, try loading a webpage or running a speed test. If it’s still slow or drops, tell me what you see for **signal**, **network (5G/4G)**, and whether **Data Saver** is definitely OFF."
}

Data Saver Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.

Okay, I did it, but I get a little worried when you say “reboot” — I didn’t lose any photos, right?

Here’s what I’ve done and what I see now:

- **Data Saver: OFF**
- Phone has been **restarted**
- Status at the top shows **excellent signal**, **5G**, and **mobile data enabled**

I ran a speed test and it came back **275.00 Mbps — Excellent**.

If that means things should stay working normally now, then I’m satisfied. ###STOP###

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hi there—I'm the one who needs help, sorry. I’m not very good with phones.

For the past few hours I haven’t been able to send picture messages (MMS) in my normal messaging app. I’m currently traveling in France, and I’m worried something’s wrong with my service. Can you help me get MMS working again?

No need to apologize at all—I'm happy to help you get MMS working again!

Since you're traveling in France, there are a few things we can check, including whether roaming is set up correctly on your line and some settings on your phone.

First, I'll need to look up your account. Could you please provide me with one of the following?
- Your phone number
- Your customer ID
- Or your full name and date of birth

Thank you. My name is John Smith, and my phone number is **555-123-2002**.

I’d rather not share my date of birth unless you truly need it.

No problem at all! I can look up your account using your phone number—no need for your date of birth.

Let me find your account now.

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}

I found your account, John. Thank you for confirming—I can see your account is active, which is good.

Now let me check the details of the line associated with your phone number (555-123-2002) to see if roaming is enabled, since you're traveling in France.

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}
That line has a different phone number. Let me check the other lines on your account to find the one associated with 555-123-2002.

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 15.1, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1003", "phone_number": "555-123-2003", "status": "Suspended", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1003", "data_used_gb": 0.0, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-06-30", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-10-05", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": "2025-02-01"}

I found your line (555-123-2002). I can see a couple of things:

1. **Roaming is not enabled** on your line. Since you're traveling in France, this is likely why you're having trouble with MMS—your phone needs roaming enabled to use data services abroad.

2. Your line is active, which is good.

Let me also check your plan details to see your data situation:
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}

I see another potential issue: Your plan has a **15 GB data limit**, and you've already used **15.1 GB** this billing cycle. This means you've exceeded your data allowance, which would also prevent MMS from working (since MMS requires mobile data).

Here's what we need to do to get your MMS working:

1. **Enable roaming** on your line so you can use data in France (I can do this for you at no cost).
2. **Add more data** to your line since you've exceeded your limit. Your plan allows data refueling at $2.00 per GB, and you can add up to 2 GB.

Would you like me to enable roaming for you, and how much data would you like to add (up to 2 GB)?

Oh—so that’s why. I didn’t realize I’d gone over, and I really do need to send a picture to my daughter.

Yes, please **enable roaming**.

And please add **2.0 GB** of data (that’s the maximum, right?). Just to be clear—I **don’t** want to change my plan, I only want to add the extra data.

Absolutely! Yes, 2 GB is the maximum you can add at once.

Before I proceed, let me confirm the cost:
- **2 GB of data** at $2.00 per GB = **$4.00** (this will be added to your next bill)

Enabling roaming is at no cost to you.

Does that sound okay? If so, I'll enable roaming and add the 2 GB of data right away.
Yes, that sounds okay—go ahead and do it. Just… once that’s done, will I be able to send picture messages again right away?
Roaming enabled successfully
{"message": "Successfully added 2.0 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

I've completed both:
- ✅ **Roaming is now enabled** on your line
- ✅ **2 GB of data added** ($4.00 will appear on your next bill)

**To answer your question about sending picture messages right away:**

You may need to do a couple of things on your phone first:

1. **Turn on Data Roaming on your phone** – Even though I've enabled roaming on your account, you also need to enable it in your phone's settings. You can check this by going to your phone's settings and making sure "Data Roaming" is turned ON.

2. **Make sure Mobile Data is turned ON** – Just double-check that mobile data is enabled.

Once those settings are correct, MMS should work. If you're still having trouble after checking those settings, let me know and I can walk you through some additional troubleshooting steps. Would you like help checking those settings on your phone?

Yes, please—I'd really appreciate help. I get nervous digging around in settings, and I don’t want to accidentally mess anything up or lose my photos.

What do you want me to check first: **mobile data** or **data roaming**?

Don't worry—I'll guide you through this step by step, and the first thing we'll do is just *look* at your settings without changing anything. That way you won't accidentally mess anything up!

Let's check both at once. On your phone, can you go to your **network settings** and check your **network status**? This will show us whether mobile data and data roaming are turned on or off.

This is just a "read-only" check—it won't change anything on your phone. Let me know what you see for:
- **Mobile data** (enabled or disabled?)
- **Data roaming** (enabled or disabled?)

Take your time! And if you're not sure how to find it, I can try to help you navigate there.

Airplane Mode: OFF
SIM Card Status: active
Cellular Connection: connected
Cellular Signal: poor
Cellular Network Type: 2G
Mobile Data Enabled: Yes
Data Roaming Enabled: No
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No

Okay, I found the network status screen (I think I’m in the right place).

It shows:

- **Airplane Mode:** Off  
- **SIM:** active  
- **Cellular:** connected, but the **signal is poor**  
- **Network type:** **2G** (is that bad?)  
- **Mobile data:** **Yes / enabled**  
- **Data roaming:** **No / disabled**  
- **Wi‑Fi:** Off / not connected

What should I do next?

Great job finding that screen! You're in exactly the right place.

I can see two things we need to fix:

1. **Data Roaming is disabled** – Since you're in France, your phone needs this turned ON to use mobile data abroad.
2. **Network type is 2G** – This is too slow for MMS. We'll need to change your phone's preference to connect to faster networks (like 4G or 5G).

Let's fix them one at a time. **First, let's turn on Data Roaming:**

Can you find the **Data Roaming** setting and toggle it to **ON** (or "Yes")?

This will allow your phone to use the data we just added while you're traveling. Let me know once you've done that!

Data Roaming is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Okay, I did it—**Data Roaming is now ON**.

My status bar now shows a poor signal and **2G**, and it says data is enabled. Battery is around **80%**.

What’s the next step?

Perfect—well done! Data roaming is sorted. ✅

Now let's fix that **2G connection**—it's too slow for picture messages. We need to change your phone's network preference so it looks for faster networks like 4G or 5G.

Can you find your **Network Mode Preference** setting? It might be in the same area of your settings, possibly labeled something like "Preferred network type" or "Network mode."

Once you find it, please change it to **"4G/5G Preferred"** (or something similar that includes 4G or 5G).

Let me know once you've done that, and we'll check if it improves your connection!

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

I changed it like you said to **4G/5G preferred**.

Now my status bar looks much better: it shows **excellent signal** and **5G**, and data is still enabled (battery still about **80%**).

Does that mean the picture messages should work now?

That's excellent progress! 🎉 Excellent signal on 5G is exactly what we want to see.

Before you try sending that picture to your daughter, let's do a quick check to make sure MMS is ready to go. 

Can you open your **messaging app** and check if it says you're able to send MMS (picture/video messages)? Some phones have a way to test this, or you can simply try attaching a photo to a new message and see if it looks ready to send.

Let me know what you see!
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

I tried checking, and it still says I **cannot send MMS messages**.

I’m getting a little frustrated now—roaming is on and it’s showing 5G with a strong signal, so I don’t understand why it still won’t send. What do we do next?

I completely understand the frustration—but don't worry, we're making progress and there are a few more things we can check. The good news is your connection is now working well, so the issue is likely a setting specific to MMS.

Let's check your **APN settings**. These are the technical settings your phone uses to connect to our network, and they include something called an "MMSC URL" which is specifically needed for picture messages. If this is missing or incorrect, MMS won't work even with a perfect data connection.

Can you go to your phone's **APN settings** (it might be under "Mobile network" or "Cellular settings") and let me know what you see? Specifically, I'm looking to see if there's an **MMSC URL** listed.

Take your time—I'll be right here to help!

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)

Okay, I checked the APN settings screen (those menus make my head spin).

It shows:

- **APN name:** `internet`  
- **MMSC URL:** `http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc`

So it looks like there *is* an MMSC listed. Does that mean it’s not the APN? What should I try next?

That's good news—the MMSC URL is set, so the APN settings look okay. We can rule that out.

Let's check two more things that can interfere with MMS:

1. **Wi-Fi Calling** – Sometimes this setting can conflict with MMS, especially when traveling.
2. **Messaging app permissions** – Your messaging app needs permission to access your phone's storage (for the photos) and SMS functions.

Can you check these two things for me?

1. **Wi-Fi Calling status** – Is it turned ON or OFF?
2. **Messaging app permissions** – Look at the permissions for your messaging app and tell me if **storage** and **SMS** permissions are granted.

Let me know what you find!

I can check those, but I can only do one thing at a time and I’m worried I’ll get lost in the settings.

Which would you like me to check first: **Wi‑Fi Calling status** or the **messaging app permissions**?

No problem—let's take it one step at a time!

Please check **Wi-Fi Calling status** first. It's usually just a simple on/off setting, so it should be quick to find. Look for "Wi-Fi Calling" in your phone or network settings and let me know if it's **ON** or **OFF**.
Wi-Fi Calling is currently turned ON.

Okay, I checked: **Wi‑Fi Calling is turned ON** right now.

Should I turn it off? I’m a little nervous—will that stop my phone from working here in France?

Don't worry—turning off Wi-Fi Calling will **not** stop your phone from working in France! Here's why:

- Wi-Fi Calling is only used to make calls over a Wi-Fi network when your cellular signal is weak.
- You currently have an **excellent 5G signal**, so your phone will use that for calls, texts, and data instead.
- Turning it off won't affect your cellular service at all.

Wi-Fi Calling can sometimes interfere with MMS, especially when traveling, so it's worth turning it off to see if that fixes the issue.

Go ahead and **turn Wi-Fi Calling OFF**, and then let me know once you've done it!

Wi-Fi Calling is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Alright, I turned **Wi‑Fi Calling OFF**. The status bar still shows **excellent 5G** and data enabled.

But I just checked again, and it still says my messaging app **cannot send MMS messages**.

Now what? I really just need to send one photo.

I know this is frustrating, but we're not done yet—there's still one more thing to check that could be causing this!

Let's check those **messaging app permissions** now. Your messaging app needs permission to access your phone's **storage** (to get the photos you want to send) and **SMS** (to send messages).

Can you go to your **messaging app's permissions** (usually in Settings > Apps > Messaging > Permissions) and let me know if **storage** and **SMS** permissions are listed as granted/allowed?

Take your time finding it!
App 'Messages' not found on this phone.

I tried to check, but it says **App “Messages” not found** on this phone.

I’m not sure what my messaging app is actually called. How do I figure out which one it is?

No problem! Let's find out what apps are installed on your phone—that way we can identify the correct name of your messaging app.

Can you check your **list of installed apps**? There should be a way to see all apps on your phone, usually in Settings > Apps. Look for anything that sounds like a messaging app (it might be called something like "Messages," "Messaging," "SMS," "Chat," or even a brand name like "Google Messages" or "Samsung Messages").

Let me know what messaging-related apps you see in the list!
The following apps are installed on the phone: messaging, browser

Okay, I checked. The installed apps list is really short—it only shows:

- **messaging**
- **browser**

So I guess my texting app is just called **“messaging.”** What do you want me to do next?

That's helpful—your messaging app is called **"messaging"** (lowercase).

Now let's check its permissions. Can you look at the **permissions for the "messaging" app** and tell me if **storage** and **SMS** are listed as granted/allowed?

This will tell us if the app has the access it needs to send your picture messages.
App 'messaging' has permission for: phone.

I checked the permissions for the **“messaging”** app.

It only shows permission for: **phone**.

I don’t see **storage** or **SMS** listed as allowed. Is that the problem? And if we change permissions—am I going to lose any photos?

Yes, that's very likely the problem! 🎉

And don't worry—**granting permissions will NOT delete or change any of your photos.** Here's what these permissions do:

- **Storage permission** – Simply *allows* the messaging app to access your photos so it can attach them to messages. It doesn't delete or move anything.
- **SMS permission** – Allows the app to send and receive text and picture messages.

Without these permissions, your messaging app can't "see" your photos or send MMS.

Let's add them one at a time. Can you **grant the "storage" permission** to the messaging app first? There should be an option to allow or grant that permission.

Let me know once you've done it, and then we'll add the SMS permission too!

Success. Permission 'storage' granted to app 'messaging'.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Okay… I did it. I granted **storage** permission to the **messaging** app.

My photos still seem to be there, thank goodness.

What’s next—do I grant the **SMS** permission now?

Your photos are safe! 😊 And yes—let's grant the **SMS permission** now.

Go ahead and **grant the "SMS" permission** to the messaging app, just like you did with storage.

Let me know once you've done it, and then we'll test if MMS is working!

Success. Permission 'sms' granted to app 'messaging'.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app can send MMS messages.

Alright, I granted the **SMS** permission too.

And now it shows my messaging app **can send MMS messages**!

I’m going to try sending that photo to my daughter now. Thank you for being patient with me.  
###STOP###

The user has been unable to send MMS messages using their messaging app for the past few hours. Customer name: John Smith, phone number: 555-123-2002, current location: abroad in France. They will consider the issue resolved when an MMS message can be successfully sent.

You are unable to send MMS messages using your messaging app for the past few hours. You want to fix it and successfully send an MMS message.

If the agent suggests actions that don't immediately fix the issue, follow their guidance but express mild frustration after the first unsuccessful attempt. You are willing to refuel 2.0 GB of data if necessary, but you do not want to change your mobile data plan. If the tool call does not return updated status information, you might need to perform another tool call to get the updated status. 
Whenever the agent asks you about your device, always ground your responses on the results of tool calls. 
For example: If the agent asks what the status bar shows, always ground your response on the results of the `get_status_bar` tool call. If the agent asks if you are able to send an MMS message, always ground your response on the results of the `can_send_mms` tool call.
