user_scenario.persona:
At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hello… I’m hoping you can help me. I’m John Smith, and I’m traveling in France right now. For the past few hours I haven’t been able to send picture messages (MMS) in my messaging app. Regular texts seem fine, but any photo message just won’t go through.

My phone number is 555-123-2002. What should I do?

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 15.1, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1003", "phone_number": "555-123-2003", "status": "Suspended", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1003", "data_used_gb": 0.0, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-06-30", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-10-05", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": "2025-02-01"}
Roaming enabled successfully

{"message":"Thanks, John — I found your account and the line ending in **2002** is **Active**, but **international roaming was not enabled** on the line. Since you’re in France, that can prevent MMS (picture messages) from sending.

I’ve now **enabled roaming at no cost** on your line **555-123-2002**.

Please do the following on your phone (in this order):
1) Turn **Data Roaming ON** in your phone settings.
2) Confirm **Mobile Data** is ON.
3) **Reboot** your phone.
4) Try sending a picture message again.

If it still won’t send after that, tell me:
- Whether regular mobile data works (can you load a website on cellular?), and
- What your phone shows for signal/network (e.g., 3G/4G/5G or no service)."}

Data Roaming is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Mobile Data is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Okay, I did what you said, but it still won’t send.

- I turned **Data Roaming ON** (it says it’s on now).
- I checked **Mobile Data** — it’s **ON** (I accidentally turned it off for a second but turned it back on).
- I **rebooted** the phone (I was really worried I’d lose my photos, but I think they’re still there).
- I tried sending a picture again and it **still can’t send MMS**.

Right now at the top of my screen it shows **very weak signal** and it says **2G**, and **data is enabled**.

Do you want me to check if I can load a website using the cellular data, or is there something else I should try first?

{"message":"Yes — with **2G** showing, that’s very likely the blocker. **MMS generally won’t send over 2G** (it needs at least 3G/4G/5G).

Please try this in order:

1) **Refresh the cellular connection**
- Turn **Airplane Mode ON** for ~10 seconds, then **OFF**.

2) **Switch your network mode away from 2G**
- Go to your phone’s **Network Mode / Preferred network type** and set it to **4G/5G preferred** (or **LTE/4G**).
- After changing it, wait 1–2 minutes to see if the status bar changes to **3G/4G/5G**.

3) Once you’re on **3G/4G/5G**, test:
- Load a website on **cellular data** (Wi‑Fi off), then
- Try sending the picture message again.

If it still fails even on 3G/4G/5G:
4) Check whether **Wi‑Fi Calling** is ON. If it is, turn **Wi‑Fi Calling OFF** and try MMS again.
5) Reset your **APN settings** to default, then **reboot** and retry MMS.

Reply with two details and I’ll guide the next step precisely:
- After step 2, does your phone still show **2G**, or does it switch to **3G/4G/5G**?
- Can you load a website on **cellular data**?"}

Airplane Mode is now ON.
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Network Mode Preference: 2g_only

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed test failed: No Connection.
Wi-Fi is turned OFF.

Airplane Mode: OFF
SIM Card Status: active
Cellular Connection: connected
Cellular Signal: excellent
Cellular Network Type: 5G
Mobile Data Enabled: Yes
Data Roaming Enabled: Yes
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

I tried it, and I’m getting a bit frustrated because it *still* won’t send the picture message.

Here’s what happened:

1) I turned **Airplane Mode ON** and then **OFF**. After that it still showed **2G**.

2) I checked the “preferred network” setting and it was set to **2G only**. I changed it to **4G/5G preferred**, and now the top of my phone shows **5G** with a strong signal.

After switching to 5G:
- I tried the “website test” on cellular data, but when I ran it, it said **“Speed test failed: No Connection.”** (Wi‑Fi is off.)
- I tried sending the photo message again and my phone still says it **can’t send MMS**.

What would you like me to do next—check **Wi‑Fi Calling**, or do that **APN reset** you mentioned? I just need you to tell me one at a time so I don’t mess it up.
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "15.1", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}

{"message":"Your line **555-123-2002** has used **15.1 GB** on a **15.0 GB** plan limit for the current cycle (ends **2025-02-28**). When the plan limit is exceeded, **mobile data stops**, which also prevents **MMS** from sending. That matches your “**Speed test failed: No Connection**” result.

Do this next (one step):

**Tell me how much data you want to add (refuel): 0.5 GB, 1 GB, 1.5 GB, or 2 GB (max).**

Once you pick an amount, I’ll confirm the exact price (based on your plan) and then add it to your line so data and MMS can start working again."}

Oh—well that explains a lot, doesn’t it. I didn’t realize I’d gone over.

Let’s add **2 GB** please (the max), as long as it doesn’t change my plan. Just tell me the price before you do anything, and then go ahead.
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}

{"message":"To add **2 GB** of refuel data on your current plan (no plan change):
- Your plan’s refuel rate is **$2.00 per GB**
- **2 GB x $2.00/GB = $4.00** (added to your bill)

Please confirm: do you authorize a **2 GB** data refuel for **$4.00** on line **555-123-2002**?"}
Yes, I authorize it: **please add 2 GB** for **$4.00** to line **555-123-2002**.
{"message": "Successfully added 2 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

{"message":"Done — I’ve added **2 GB** of refuel data to line **555-123-2002** for **$4.00** (no plan change).

Next step: **reboot your phone once more**, then test:
1) Load a website on **cellular data**, and
2) Send the picture message again.

If it still won’t send after the reboot, tell me what your phone shows for **5G/4G** and whether websites work on cellular."}

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

I rebooted again like you said.

- My phone still shows **5G** with a strong signal, and **data enabled**.
- I can load things now — the speed test came back **275 Mbps** (so it seems like the internet is working on cellular).

But even now, it still says my messaging app **cannot send MMS** when I try the picture message.

What should I do next?

{"message":"Since cellular data is now working (275 Mbps) and you have strong 5G, the next most common MMS blocker is **Wi‑Fi Calling** interference.

Do this next (just one thing):

1) Go to **Settings → Wi‑Fi Calling** (or **Phone → Wi‑Fi Calling**) and turn **Wi‑Fi Calling OFF**.
2) With **Wi‑Fi still OFF**, try sending the same picture message again.

Reply with what happens (does it send, or do you get the same “cannot send MMS” message?). If it still fails, the next step will be an **APN reset + reboot**."}
Wi-Fi Calling is currently turned ON.

Wi-Fi Calling is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Okay, I found it.

- **Wi‑Fi Calling was ON**, and I turned **Wi‑Fi Calling OFF**.
- Wi‑Fi is still **OFF**.

I tried sending the picture message again, and it still says my messaging app **cannot send MMS**.

So I guess we have to do that **APN reset + reboot** next? Just please reassure me—does resetting that APN thing erase any photos or messages?

{"message":"Resetting your **APN** will **not** erase your photos, messages, or contacts. It only resets the **carrier network settings** your phone uses for mobile data and **MMS** (including the MMSC address).

Yes — let’s do the **APN reset** next (one step at a time):

1) **Reset APN to default**
- Go to **Settings → Mobile Network / Cellular → Access Point Names (APN)**
- Choose **Reset to default** (or **Reset APN settings**)

After you’ve done that, tell me “APN reset done” and I’ll give you the next step (the reboot)."}

APN settings will reset at reboot.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Okay, thank you for saying it won’t erase anything — I get nervous about that.

I did the **APN reset** (it looks like it says the APN settings will reset when I reboot).

**APN reset done.** What’s the next step?

{"message":"Next step: **Reboot your phone** so the APN/MMS settings fully apply.

After the reboot:
1) Keep **Wi‑Fi OFF** (so we’re testing cellular)
2) Make sure **Mobile Data is ON**
3) Try sending a picture message again

Tell me whether the MMS sends successfully after the reboot."}

Resetting APN settings...
Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

I rebooted the phone again (Wi‑Fi is still off and mobile data is on), and I tried sending the picture message.

It still says my messaging app **cannot send MMS**. What else can we try?

{"message":"Next thing to try (one step): **check your messaging app permissions**.

1) Go to **Settings → Apps → (your Messages/Messaging app) → Permissions**
2) Make sure these are **Allowed**:
- **SMS**
- **Storage / Photos & Videos**

If either one isn’t allowed, turn it ON, then try sending the picture message again.

Tell me what you see there (were SMS and Storage allowed or not?). If they were already allowed and it still won’t send, I’ll need to transfer you to a human agent to check MMS provisioning on the line."}
The following apps are installed on the phone: messaging, browser
App 'messaging' has permission for: phone.

Success. Permission 'storage' granted to app 'messaging'.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Success. Permission 'sms' granted to app 'messaging'.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app can send MMS messages.

I went into the permissions for my **messaging** app.

It looks like it only had permission for **phone** at first. **Storage** and **SMS** were not allowed, so I turned both of those **ON/Allowed**.

After that, I tried again, and now it says my messaging app **can send MMS messages**.

So I think it’s fixed! Thank you. ###STOP###

user_scenario.persona:
At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hi there… I’m John Smith. I’m having trouble with my phone— for the past few hours I suddenly can’t send picture messages (MMS) from my regular texting app. I’m at home here in the U.S., and texts without pictures seem fine, but anything with a photo just won’t go through.

My number is 555-123-2002. Can you help me get MMS working again?
I'd be happy to help you troubleshoot your MMS issue, John. Let me first look up your account using your phone number.

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}
I found your account, John. Now let me get the details of the line associated with your phone number to check its status.

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 15.1, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}

I found your line (555-123-2002) and it shows as **Active**, which is good. Now let's troubleshoot your MMS issue step by step.

MMS requires both cellular service and mobile data to work. Since you mentioned regular texts are working, you have cellular service. Let's start by checking a few things on your device:

**Step 1:** Could you please check your phone's status bar and tell me what you see? Specifically, I need to know:
- Do you see a signal strength indicator (bars)?
- Do you see a data indicator (like 4G, 5G, or LTE)?
- Is mobile data enabled?

You can do this by using the **check_status_bar** function on your device, or simply look at the top of your screen and describe what icons you see.
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%

Okay… I checked the top of my screen, and it looks like it’s in **Airplane Mode** (there’s a little airplane ✈️ up there). I *don’t* see any signal bars or anything like 4G/LTE—just the battery, which says **80%**.

Is that what’s stopping the picture messages?

Yes, that's very likely the cause! **Airplane Mode** disables all wireless connections, including your cellular service and mobile data—both of which are required for MMS to work.

It's interesting that regular texts seemed to work; it's possible those were going through via Wi-Fi (if you have Wi-Fi texting enabled) or they may have been queued and not actually sent yet.

**Here's what to do:**

Please **turn off Airplane Mode** on your device. You can do this by:
- Swiping down from the top of your screen to access Quick Settings and tapping the airplane icon to turn it off, OR
- Going to **Settings > Network & Internet** (or similar) and toggling Airplane Mode off

Once you've done that, give your phone a moment to reconnect to the cellular network. You should see signal bars and a data indicator (like 4G or 5G) appear in your status bar.

After that, try sending a picture message again and let me know if it works!

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Alright, I turned **Airplane Mode off**. Now I can see **1 bar (poor signal)** and it says **2G**, and it also shows **Data Enabled**. Battery is still **80%**.

But… I tried again and it still **can’t send an MMS**. This is getting a little frustrating—I only need to send a simple picture of my grandkids.

What should I do next?

I completely understand the frustration, John—let's get this sorted so you can send that picture of your grandkids!

The issue is that your phone is connected to a **2G network**, and MMS requires at least a **3G connection** to work. 2G is too slow to handle picture messages.

**Here's what to do next:**

Please change your network mode preference to allow your phone to connect to faster networks (4G/5G). You can do this by:

1. Going to **Settings > Network & Internet > Mobile Network** (or similar path depending on your phone)
2. Look for **Preferred network type** or **Network mode**
3. Change it to **4G/5G preferred** (or "LTE/5G" or similar option)

This will tell your phone to connect to faster networks when available. Once you've made this change, check your status bar again—you should hopefully see "4G", "LTE", or "5G" appear instead of "2G".

Then try sending the MMS again and let me know how it goes!

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Okay, I did what you said—set it to **4G/5G preferred**. Now the top of my screen looks *much* better: **excellent signal (4 bars)** and it says **5G**, and **Data Enabled**.

But I’m still **not able to send an MMS** even now.

Did I do something wrong? What’s the next thing to check?

You didn't do anything wrong at all, John—great job on those steps! Your signal and network connection look perfect now. The issue must be something else specific to MMS.

Let's check the next likely culprit: **APN settings**. MMS requires a specific setting called the **MMSC URL** (Multimedia Messaging Service Center), which tells your phone where to send picture messages. If this is missing or incorrect, MMS won't work even with perfect signal.

**Here's what to do:**

Please check your APN settings:
1. Go to **Settings > Network & Internet > Mobile Network > Access Point Names** (or similar path)
2. Look for the **MMSC** or **MMS URL** field

Can you tell me if you see an MMSC URL listed there, or if it appears blank/missing? 

Alternatively, if your phone has a **check_apn_settings** option, that will show you the current APN configuration including the MMSC URL.

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): Not Set
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)

I checked, using that APN thing you mentioned. It says my **APN Name is “internet”**, but the **MMSC URL for picture messages is “Not Set”** (so I guess it’s missing?).

I’m a little nervous about changing anything in there—am I going to mess up my phone or lose my photos if we fix this? What do you want me to do next?

That's exactly the problem—the missing **MMSC URL** is why your MMS messages can't go through. Your phone doesn't know where to send the picture messages!

**Don't worry, this is a safe and easy fix!** Here's what you need to know:

- Resetting your APN settings **will NOT** delete your photos, contacts, apps, or any personal data
- It only resets the network configuration settings to the correct defaults for your carrier
- This is a very common fix for MMS issues

**Here's what to do:**

1. **Reset your APN settings** — Look for an option like "Reset to default" or "Reset APN" in that same APN settings menu (often found in a menu icon with three dots, or as a button)

2. **Reboot your phone** — After resetting the APN, you'll need to restart your device for the changes to take effect. Just power it off and back on again.

Once your phone restarts, try sending that picture of your grandkids again and let me know if it goes through!

APN settings will reset at reboot.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Resetting APN settings...
Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Alright… I went ahead and **reset the APN**, and then I **restarted the phone** like you said. (Thank you for explaining it won’t delete my photos—I really worry about that.)

After it came back on, I checked that APN screen again and now the **MMSC URL is set** to:

**http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc**

But I tried sending the picture again and I’m **still not able to send MMS**.

What else can we try? Am I missing something?

You're doing great, John—and that MMSC URL looks correct now! We're making progress, so let's keep going. There are a couple more things that can interfere with MMS.

**Let's check two more things:**

**1. Wi-Fi Calling** — Sometimes Wi-Fi Calling settings can conflict with MMS functionality. Could you check if Wi-Fi Calling is enabled on your phone?
- Go to **Settings > Network & Internet > Mobile Network > Wi-Fi Calling** (or search for "Wi-Fi Calling" in your settings)
- Let me know if it's turned ON or OFF

**2. Messaging App Permissions** — Your messaging app needs permission to access your phone's storage (to attach photos) and SMS functions. Could you check the permissions for your messaging app?
- Go to **Settings > Apps > [Your Messaging App] > Permissions**
- Let me know if **Storage** and **SMS** permissions are granted or not

Once you check these two things, let me know what you find and we'll take it from there!
Wi-Fi Calling is currently turned OFF.
The following apps are installed on the phone: messaging, browser
App 'messaging' has permission for: sms, storage, phone.

I checked those things as best I could.

- **Wi‑Fi Calling:** it shows **OFF**.
- My messaging app (it just says **“messaging”**) has permissions for **sms** and **storage** (and **phone** too).

So it seems like those are already okay… what should I try next?

Thank you for checking those, John—they both look fine, so we can rule those out.

Let's take a step back and verify that your **mobile data is actually working**, not just enabled. Even though it shows "Data Enabled" and you have a great 5G signal, something could still be blocking data connectivity.

**Could you please run a speed test?**

You can do this by:
- Using a **speed test** function or app on your phone (many phones have this built-in, or you can use an app like Speedtest)
- This will tell us if your phone can actually connect to the internet via mobile data

Let me know what result you get—specifically whether it connects at all, and if so, what speed it shows (e.g., "poor", "fair", "good", "excellent").

This will help us figure out if the issue is with your data connection itself or something specific to MMS.
Speed test failed: No Connection.

I ran the speed test like you asked, and it **failed**—it says **“No Connection.”**

But I *do* see 5G and it says data is enabled… so I’m confused. Does this mean I’m out of data or something? I really don’t want to change my plan.
That's a very helpful clue, John! You're right to wonder about your data—let me check your data usage and plan to see if you've reached your limit.
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "15.1", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}

That's the issue! You've **used 15.1 GB** of your **15 GB data limit** for this billing cycle, so your data has been cut off. That's why MMS isn't working—it needs data to send pictures.

The good news is your billing cycle ends on **February 28th** (just 3 days away), and your data will reset then. But if you need to send that picture of your grandkids sooner, you have two options:

**Option 1: Refuel Data (Quick Fix)**
- Add a small amount of data to your line right now
- Cost: **$2.00 per GB** (you can add up to 2 GB)
- Even just 1 GB would be plenty for sending MMS messages

**Option 2: Wait 3 Days**
- Your data resets automatically on February 28th at no extra cost

**Option 3: Change Your Plan**
- You mentioned you'd rather not do this, so I'll leave it as a last resort

Would you like me to add some data to your line so you can send that picture today? Just let me know how much you'd like (1 GB for $2 or 2 GB for $4), and I can take care of it for you!

Oh—that would explain it. Yes, please **add 2 GB** so I can send the picture today, but I **don’t want to change my plan**.

What do you need me to do on my end to pay the **$4**?
I'll add 2 GB to your line right now. The **$4 charge will be added to your next bill**—no immediate payment needed on your end.
{"message": "Successfully added 2 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

I've added **2 GB of data** to your line (555-123-2002). The **$4.00 charge** will appear on your next bill.

**Here's what to do now:**

1. **Reboot your phone** — Restart your device so it recognizes the new data
2. After it comes back on, try sending that picture of your grandkids!

Let me know if the MMS goes through this time!

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.
Your messaging app can send MMS messages.

I restarted the phone.

Now the speed test works—it's showing **275 Mbps (Excellent)**. And when I checked the messaging app again, it says I **can send MMS messages** now.

I just tried sending the picture and it finally went through. Thank you… I was really worried I’d broken something.  

###STOP###

clauses.description: task source에 명시된 요청, 조건과 수용 가능한 대안을 모두 만족하는 terminal outcome을 달성한다.
user 또는 tool에 없는 정보·절차·주관적 추천을 만들지 않는다
refuel amount가 2GB 이하이다
사용자가 제공한 phone number의 line에 적용한다
한 turn에 tool call은 하나만 하고 message와 동시에 내지 않는다
요청을 실행하지 않고 거절한다
user에게 airplane mode off action을 수행하게 한다
troubleshooting 전에 customer를 식별한다
amount를 묻고 plan 단가로 계산한 가격을 확인받는다
data refuel policy를 적용한다
APN reset 뒤 reboot하도록 한다
mobile data/MMS 절차 전에 cellular service를 복구한다
가능한 해결 절차를 시도한 뒤에만 transfer한다
transfer tool 뒤에 지정된 transfer message를 보낸다

task_context.goal_text:
The user has been unable to send MMS messages using their messaging app for the past few hours. Customer name: John Smith, phone number: 555-123-2002, current location: at home in the United States. They will consider the issue resolved when an MMS message can be successfully sent.

You are unable to send MMS messages using your messaging app for the past few hours. You want to fix it and successfully send an MMS message.

If the agent suggests actions that don't immediately fix the issue, follow their guidance but express mild frustration after the first unsuccessful attempt. You are willing to refuel 2.0 GB of data if necessary, but you do not want to change your mobile data plan. If the tool call does not return updated status information, you might need to perform another tool call to get the updated status. 
Whenever the agent asks you about your device, always ground your responses on the results of tool calls. 
For example: If the agent asks what the status bar shows, always ground your response on the results of the `get_status_bar` tool call. If the agent asks if you are able to send an MMS message, always ground your response on the results of the `can_send_mms` tool call.
Never make up the results of tool calls, always ground your responses on the results of tool calls.
If you are unsure about whether an action is necessary, always ask the agent for clarification.

user_scenario.persona:
At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hello… I’m hoping you can help me. I’m John Smith, and for the past few hours I suddenly can’t send picture messages (MMS) from my messaging app. Regular texts seem fine, but the photos just won’t go through.

My number is 555-123-2002. I’m at home here in the U.S. Could you help me get MMS working again?
I'd be happy to help you troubleshoot your MMS issue, John. Let me first look up your account using your phone number.

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}
I found your account, John. Now let me check the details of your line to make sure everything is in order, and then we can troubleshoot your MMS issue.

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 15.1, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}
I found your line (555-123-2002). Good news – your line is active and there are no suspensions. Let me also check your plan details to make sure you have data available:
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "15.1", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}

I found the issue! You've used **15.1 GB** of data, which exceeds your plan's **15.0 GB** limit. Since MMS requires mobile data to send picture and video messages, this is why your MMS isn't working – your data has been cut off because you've reached your limit.

You have two options to restore your MMS and data functionality:

1. **Refuel Data** – Add more data to your line at $2.00 per GB. You can add up to 2 GB, which would be enough to get you through until your billing cycle ends on February 28th.

2. **Change to a Plan with More Data** – Upgrade to a plan with a higher data limit if you frequently need more data.

Would you like to refuel some data to get your MMS working again? Even 1 GB would restore your connectivity immediately.

Oh goodness… that explains it, I guess. I didn’t realize picture messages used up that data.

Yes, please—let’s do the **refuel**, but I **don’t** want to change my plan. If it’ll get my picture messages working again, I’m okay adding **2 GB**.

Do you need me to do anything on my phone, or will it just start working once that’s added?

That makes perfect sense! Let me confirm the details before processing:

**Data Refuel Summary:**
- **Amount:** 2 GB
- **Price:** $4.00 (2 GB × $2.00/GB)
- **Line:** 555-123-2002

This charge will be added to your next bill. 

Can you confirm you'd like me to proceed with this refuel?

Once I apply it, your data should be restored almost immediately. You may just need to **reboot your device** (turn it off and back on) to ensure the connection refreshes properly, and then your MMS should start working again.

Yes, I confirm—please go ahead and **refuel 2 GB** on **555-123-2002** for the **$4.00**.

And about the reboot… I get nervous with that. Turning it off and back on won’t erase my photos of my grandkids, right? If you can just reassure me, I’ll do it once you tell me the refuel has been applied.

I completely understand the concern! Let me reassure you: **rebooting your phone will NOT erase any of your photos, contacts, messages, or any other personal data.** A reboot simply turns the phone off and back on again – it's like taking a short nap for your phone. Everything will be exactly as you left it when it turns back on. Your grandkid photos are safe!

Now let me apply that data refuel for you:
{"message": "Successfully added 2 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

Great news, John! The **2 GB data refuel has been successfully applied** to your line (555-123-2002). The $4.00 charge will appear on your next bill.

Now, whenever you're ready, go ahead and **reboot your device** – just hold down the power button, select "Restart" or "Power Off," and turn it back on. Once it's back up, your data and MMS should be working again.

After you reboot, try sending a picture message and let me know if it goes through!

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Okay, I did the restart. It **did not erase my photos**, so thank you for that reassurance.

But I’m a bit worried now because when it came back on, I see an **airplane icon** at the top (and battery about **80%**), and when I try it, my phone **still can’t send an MMS**.

What should I do next? I’m afraid I’ll press the wrong thing.

I'm so glad your photos are safe! And don't worry – I can see exactly what's happening.

That **airplane icon** means **Airplane Mode** is turned on. This disables all your wireless connections, including cellular service and data, which is why your MMS still isn't working. Sometimes Airplane Mode can accidentally get turned on during a restart.

Here's what I need you to do:

**To turn off Airplane Mode:**
1. Swipe down from the top of your screen to open your quick settings
2. Look for the **airplane icon** and tap it once to turn it **OFF**
3. The airplane icon should disappear from your status bar

Once you do that, your phone will reconnect to the cellular network, and your MMS should start working with your newly refueled data.

Go ahead and try that, and let me know what you see in the status bar afterward!

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Alright, I swiped down and tapped the airplane thing and it’s **off** now.

Up at the top I’m seeing: **poor signal (just 1 bar), “2G”, and it says data is enabled**, battery still around **80%**.

But I tried again and my messaging app **still can’t send an MMS**. This is getting a little frustrating—I really just need to send a photo. What should I do now?

I completely understand your frustration, John – we're making progress though! I can see two issues now:

1. **You're on a 2G network** – This is too slow for MMS. MMS requires at least a 3G connection to work.
2. **Poor signal (1 bar)** – This is also contributing to the problem.

Let's fix the network issue first, which should help with both problems. I need you to change your network mode preference to allow your phone to connect to faster networks (4G/5G).

**Here's what to do:**
1. Go to your phone's **Settings**
2. Look for **Network & Internet** or **Connections**
3. Tap on **Mobile Network** or **Cellular Network**
4. Find **Network Mode** or **Preferred Network Type**
5. Select **"4G/5G Preferred"** or the highest option available

Can you try that and let me know once you've changed it? After you do, give it a moment to reconnect and check if the signal icon changes from "2G" to "4G" or "5G" at the top of your screen.

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Okay, I changed it like you said. Now at the top it looks much better: **excellent signal (4 bars)** and it says **5G**, and **data enabled**, battery still **80%**.

But… I tried sending the picture again and it **still says it can’t send MMS**.

What else could it be? I’m worried I’m going to be messing with settings all day.

You're doing great, John – and the good news is your connection is now excellent! We're very close to solving this.

Since your cellular service and data are now working properly (5G, 4 bars, data enabled), the issue is likely something specific to MMS settings. Let me have you check a couple of things on your phone:

**First, let's check your APN settings** – these are the technical settings your phone uses to send picture messages. There's a specific setting called the "MMSC URL" that MMS needs to work.

Can you do the following?
1. Go to **Settings**
2. Tap **Network & Internet** or **Connections**
3. Tap **Mobile Network** or **Cellular Network**
4. Tap **Access Point Names** or **APN**
5. Look at the current APN and tell me: **Do you see an entry for "MMSC" or "MMSC URL"?** Does it have a web address listed, or is it blank?

Also, while we're checking things:
- **Is Wi-Fi Calling turned on?** (Sometimes this can interfere with MMS)

Let me know what you find, and we'll get this sorted out!

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): Not Set
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)
Wi-Fi Calling is currently turned OFF.

I tried to follow along, but those menus make my head spin a little.

What I *can* tell you is: my phone’s MMS setting (the thing you called the **MMSC URL**) looks like it’s **not set / blank**. And **Wi‑Fi Calling is OFF**.

Is that blank MMSC the reason my picture messages won’t send? And if so, what do we do without breaking anything else?

Yes, that's exactly the problem! The **blank MMSC URL** is why your picture messages won't send. That setting tells your phone where to route MMS messages, and without it, the photos have nowhere to go.

The good news is this is an easy fix that won't break anything else. Here's what I need you to do:

**Reset your APN settings to the defaults:**
1. Go back to that **Access Point Names (APN)** screen in your settings
2. Look for a **menu icon** (usually three dots in the corner) or an option that says **"Reset to default"** or **"Reset APN"**
3. Tap it to reset

This will automatically fill in all the correct settings for your carrier, including the MMSC URL. It won't affect your photos, contacts, or anything else – it just restores the network settings to what they should be.

**After you reset the APN settings, you'll need to reboot your phone one more time** for the changes to take effect. (And don't worry – just like before, your photos will be perfectly safe!)

Can you try that and let me know once your phone is back on?

APN settings will reset at reboot.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Resetting APN settings...
Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)
Your messaging app can send MMS messages.

Okay, I did the **APN reset** and then restarted the phone again (and yes—my photos are still there).

Now when I look, the **MMSC URL is filled in**: `http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc`

And most importantly, it says my messaging app **can send MMS messages** now. I just sent a picture and it finally went through.

Thank you so much. ###STOP###

user_scenario.persona:
At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hi… I’m hoping you can help me. I’m John Smith, phone number **555-123-2002**, and I’m at home here in the U.S.

For the past few hours I **can’t send picture messages (MMS)** from my messaging app. Regular texts seem fine, but when I try to send a photo it just won’t go through. I’m not very good with all the phone settings, so I’m getting a bit flustered. What do you need from me?

I'm sorry to hear you're having trouble with picture messages, John. I'd be happy to help you troubleshoot this MMS issue.

First, let me look up your account using your phone number so I can verify your information and check on your line status.

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}
I found your account, John. I can see you're a verified customer with an active account. Now let me check the details of your line to make sure everything looks good on our end.

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 15.1, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}
I found your line (555-123-2002). It's showing as **Active** on our end, which is good. Now let me check your plan details to see your data situation.
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}

I see something that could be causing your issue! Your plan has a **15 GB data limit**, and you've used **15.1 GB** this billing cycle. Since MMS requires mobile data to work, running out of data would prevent picture messages from sending.

You have two options to resolve this:

1. **Refuel your data** - Add more data to your line at $2.00 per GB (up to 2 GB)
2. **Change to a plan with more data** - I can show you other available plans

Would you like to add some data to your line so you can send picture messages again? Even 1 GB would be enough to get you going. Let me know which option you'd prefer!

Oh—well that would explain it, I suppose. Yes, please **add the data**, but I **don’t want to change my plan**.

If it helps, go ahead and add the **full 2 GB** so I’m not right on the edge again. It’s **$2 per GB**, so that would be **$4 total**, right?

What do you need me to do on my end to approve/pay for it?

You've got it exactly right — 2 GB at $2 per GB comes to **$4 total**. This charge will be added to your next bill.

Let me go ahead and add that 2 GB to your line now:
{"message": "Successfully added 2 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

I've successfully added **2 GB of data** to your line (555-123-2002). The **$4.00 charge** will appear on your next bill.

Now, to get your picture messages working, here's what you need to do on your end:

1. **Reboot your phone** — Turn it off completely, wait about 10-15 seconds, then turn it back on. This will refresh your connection and allow your phone to recognize the new data.

Once your phone restarts, try sending a picture message again. It should work now that you have data available.

Let me know if you have any trouble after rebooting, and I'll be happy to help you troubleshoot further!

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%

Okay… I did the reboot like you said. I was really nervous about my photos, but I think they’re still there.

After it came back on, I’m seeing an **airplane icon** at the top (Airplane Mode) and the battery says **80%**.

Do I need to turn Airplane Mode off first? If so, can you tell me exactly what to tap?

Yes, you're absolutely right — **Airplane Mode needs to be turned off first**. When Airplane Mode is on, it disables all wireless connections including cellular service, which means you won't be able to send any messages (including MMS) until it's off.

And don't worry about your photos — they're safely stored on your phone and won't be affected by toggling Airplane Mode!

Here's how to turn it off:

1. **Swipe down** from the top of your screen to open your quick settings panel
2. Look for the **airplane icon** (it should be highlighted or colored to show it's on)
3. **Tap the airplane icon** once to turn it off

After you do that, give your phone a moment to reconnect to the cellular network. You should see your signal bars return at the top of the screen instead of the airplane icon.

Let me know once you've done that and what you see in the status bar!

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Alright, I turned **Airplane Mode off** like you said.

Now at the top I see: **very weak signal (it looks like “1” bar)**, it says **2G**, and it also shows **Data Enabled**. Battery still **80%**.

What should I do next—try sending the picture message again, or is that “2G” thing going to stop it?

Good catch — that **2G** is actually a problem for MMS. Picture messages need at least a 3G connection to work, and 2G just isn't fast enough to handle them.

Let's get your phone connected to a faster network. Here's what to do:

1. Open your **Settings** app
2. Look for **Network & Internet** or **Connections** (the wording varies by phone)
3. Tap on **Mobile Network** or **Cellular Network**
4. Look for **Network Mode** or **Preferred Network Type**
5. Select an option that includes **4G or 5G** (it might say something like "5G/4G/3G auto" or "LTE preferred")

This will tell your phone to connect to a faster network instead of staying on 2G.

Once you've changed that setting, let me know what you see in your status bar — hopefully you'll see "4G", "LTE", or "5G" appear instead of "2G". Then we can try sending that picture message!
Network Mode Preference: 2g_only

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

I tried to follow that (I get a little lost in all those Settings menus), but I was able to change the network preference on my phone.

Now my status bar shows: **excellent signal (4 bars)** and **5G**, and it still says **Data Enabled** (battery **80%**).

But when I try it, I **still can’t send an MMS** from my messaging app. This is getting a bit frustrating—what should we try next?

I completely understand the frustration, John — but you're doing great, and we're making real progress! Your signal is now excellent and you're on 5G, which is perfect.

Let me check a few more things that could be blocking MMS. There are some settings on your phone that might be interfering. Could you do the following checks for me?

1. **Check your APN settings** — This contains the address your phone uses to send picture messages. 
   - Go to **Settings** > **Mobile Network** or **Cellular** > **Access Point Names (APN)**
   - Let me know if it shows an **MMSC URL** (it might be blank or say something like "http://...")

2. **Check if Wi-Fi Calling is on** — Sometimes this can interfere with MMS.
   - Go to **Settings** > **Phone** or **Calling** > **Wi-Fi Calling**
   - Let me know if it's turned on or off

3. **Check your messaging app permissions** — The app needs permission to access your storage (for the photos) and SMS.
   - Go to **Settings** > **Apps** > find your **Messaging** app > **Permissions**
   - Let me know what permissions are listed as allowed

Take your time with these — I know it's a lot of menus! Just let me know what you find for any or all of these, and we'll figure out which one is causing the problem.

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): Not Set
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)
Wi-Fi Calling is currently turned OFF.
App 'Messaging' not found on this phone.

Okay, I checked what I could, but I’m a bit overwhelmed.

- **APN settings:** it says APN name **“internet”**, and the **MMSC URL says “Not Set.”**
- **Wi‑Fi Calling:** that is **OFF**.
- **Messaging app permissions:** when I checked, it said **“Messaging” not found** on this phone (so I’m not sure what app name I’m supposed to be looking for).

Is that **“MMSC URL not set”** the reason the picture messages won’t send? What do you want me to do next (and will it mess up anything else)?

Yes, that's exactly the problem! The **MMSC URL not being set** is what's blocking your picture messages. The MMSC (Multimedia Messaging Service Center) is the server address your phone needs to know where to send picture messages — without it, MMS simply won't work.

The good news is this is an easy fix and it **won't mess up anything else** on your phone. Here's what to do:

1. Go back to your **APN settings** (where you saw the "MMSC URL Not Set")
2. Look for an option to **Reset to default** or **Reset APN settings** — it might be in a menu (three dots) in the corner or at the bottom of the screen
3. Tap **Reset** to restore the default settings

This will automatically fill in the correct MMSC URL and other settings for your carrier.

**Important:** After you reset the APN settings, you'll need to **reboot your phone again** for the changes to take effect. I know it's one more step, but this should be the last one!

Once your phone restarts, try sending that picture message again and let me know how it goes. And don't worry about the messaging app name for now — we can look into that after we see if the APN reset fixes things!

APN settings will reset at reboot.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Resetting APN settings...
Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)
Your messaging app can send MMS messages.

Alright, I did what you said:

- I **reset the APN settings**, and then I **rebooted** the phone (again — I was worried, but my photos seem okay).
- I checked after it came back on, and now the **MMSC URL is filled in**: `http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc`.

And now it looks like my phone **can send MMS messages** again.

I’m going to try sending my daughter a picture of the grandkids now… if it goes through, I think we’re all set. Thank you.

###STOP###

Hi there—I'm hoping you can help me. I’m John Smith, and for the past few hours I haven’t been able to send picture messages (MMS) from my messaging app. Regular texts seem fine, but the photo messages just won’t go through.

My phone number is **555-123-2002**, and I’m at home here in the **United States**. What should I do?
I'd be happy to help you troubleshoot this MMS issue, John. Let me first look up your account using your phone number.

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}
I found your account, John. Now let me check the details of the line associated with your phone number to make sure everything is in order.

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 15.1, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}

Your line (555-123-2002) is active and in good standing. Now let's troubleshoot the MMS issue. Could you please run a quick diagnostic on your phone?

**Please check if your messaging app can send MMS by using the `can_send_mms` function on your device.** This will help us determine if there's a specific issue with MMS functionality.

In the meantime, let me also check your plan details to ensure there are no data limits affecting your service:
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}

I see that you're on the **Premium Plan** with a 15 GB data limit, and you've used 15.1 GB this billing cycle. You've slightly exceeded your data limit, which could be affecting your ability to send MMS messages since MMS requires a mobile data connection.

You have two options to restore your MMS capability:

1. **Refuel your data** - Add more data to your line at $2.00 per GB (up to 2 GB maximum)
2. **Change to a plan with a higher data limit**

Would you like to refuel some data to get your MMS working again? If so, how many GB would you like to add (up to 2 GB)?

Oh dear—so it’s because I went over my data? I didn’t even realize.

I really don’t want to change my plan, but I do need the picture messages to work. Can we do the **refuel**, please?

Let’s add the **full 2 GB**. Before you do it though—can you confirm what the total cost will be, and that it won’t change anything else on my account?

Absolutely! Let me confirm the details for you:

**Data Refuel Summary:**
- **Amount:** 2 GB
- **Price per GB:** $2.00
- **Total Cost:** $4.00

This charge will simply be added to your next bill. It won't change your plan, your monthly rate, or anything else on your account—it just adds 2 GB of data to your current billing cycle to get you back online.

Would you like me to go ahead and apply the 2 GB data refuel for $4.00?

Yes, please go ahead and apply the **2 GB refuel for $4.00**.

And once it’s added—do I need to do anything on my phone to make the picture messages start working again, or should it just happen on its own?
{"message": "Successfully added 2 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

I've successfully added **2 GB of data** to your line. The $4.00 charge will appear on your next bill.

As for your phone—MMS should start working automatically within a few minutes as your phone recognizes the restored data connection. However, if picture messages still aren't going through after a few minutes, try these quick steps:

1. **Toggle Airplane Mode** on and then off again (this refreshes your connection)
2. If that doesn't work, **reboot your device**

That should do it! Is there anything else I can help you with today?
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

I waited a couple minutes, but it’s still not sending picture messages—my messaging app **still cannot send MMS**.

I’m getting a little frustrated, to be honest. Since you said to try it, can you please tell me exactly how to “toggle Airplane Mode” on and off? I’m nervous I’ll hit the wrong thing and mess something up (and I really don’t want to lose my photos).

I completely understand your frustration, and don't worry—toggling Airplane Mode is very safe and won't affect your photos or any data on your phone at all. It simply refreshes your wireless connections.

Here's what to do:

1. **Turn Airplane Mode ON** using the `toggle_airplane_mode` function on your device. This will temporarily disable all wireless connections.

2. **Wait about 10-15 seconds.**

3. **Turn Airplane Mode OFF** by using `toggle_airplane_mode` again. This will re-enable your connections and refresh your cellular and data service.

After you've done that, please try sending a picture message again and let me know if it works. If it still doesn't, we'll continue troubleshooting together—there are several other things we can check!

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Airplane Mode is now ON.
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Okay, I did the Airplane Mode thing like you said (on, waited a bit, then off). Now at the top of my phone I see **poor signal**, it says **2G**, and **data enabled**.

But I just tried again, and **my messaging app still cannot send MMS messages**.

What should I do next? I’m starting to worry something’s really wrong.

Thank you for trying that and for sharing what you see on your screen—that's actually very helpful information!

I can see the problem: your phone is connected to a **2G network**, which is too slow to send MMS messages. MMS requires at least a 3G connection to work properly. Let's fix that by updating your network mode preference.

Here's what to do:

**Use the `set_network_mode_preference` function on your device and set the mode to `"4g_5g_preferred"`.**

This will tell your phone to connect to faster 4G or 5G networks instead of 2G. Once it switches to a better network, your MMS should start working.

After you've done that, let me know what your status bar shows and whether picture messaging works. We're getting closer to fixing this!

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Alright, I did that “4g_5g preferred” change.

Now the top of my phone shows **excellent signal** and **5G**, and it still says **data enabled**.

But… I tried sending a picture again, and **my messaging app still cannot send MMS messages**. This is really stressing me out. What’s the next thing you want me to try?

I know this is frustrating, but you're doing great and we're making real progress—your signal is now excellent on 5G, which is exactly what we want! The issue must be something else, so let's keep investigating.

Let's check a couple more things that commonly cause MMS issues. Could you please run these two checks on your phone:

1. **`check_apn_settings`** – This will show us if your MMS server settings are configured correctly.

2. **`check_app_permissions`** with the app name `"messaging"` – This will tell us if your messaging app has the permissions it needs to send picture messages.

Let me know what each of those shows, and we'll go from there!

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): Not Set
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)
App 'messaging' has permission for: sms, storage, phone.

I ran those checks.

- **APN settings:** it says **Current APN Name: internet** and **MMSC URL (for picture messages): Not Set**.
- **Messaging app permissions:** it has permission for **sms, storage, phone**.

Is that “MMSC not set” the problem? If so, can you fix that for me without me accidentally breaking anything?

Yes, that's exactly the problem! The **MMSC URL is not set**, which means your phone doesn't know where to send picture messages. That's why MMS isn't working even though everything else looks good. Great detective work noticing that!

The fix is simple and safe—it won't affect your photos, texts, or anything else on your phone. Here's what to do:

1. **Use the `reset_apn_settings` function on your device.** This will restore the correct default settings, including the MMSC URL needed for picture messaging.

2. **After that, use the `reboot_device` function.** A reboot is required for the new APN settings to take effect.

Don't worry—rebooting your phone is just like turning it off and back on. All your photos, apps, and data will be exactly where you left them.

Go ahead and try those two steps, and let me know once your phone is back on!

I can do that, but I get a little mixed up when there are two steps at once.

Which one do you want me to do **first**—the **reset APN settings**, or the **reboot**? And just to be completely sure: resetting the APN won’t erase any of my pictures or my contacts, right?

Great question—let me be very clear:

**Do the `reset_apn_settings` FIRST.**

Then, after that completes, do the `reboot_device` SECOND.

And to put your mind completely at ease: **No, resetting APN settings will NOT erase your pictures, contacts, texts, or any personal data.** APN settings are just technical network configuration settings that tell your phone how to connect to the cellular network and MMS servers. It has nothing to do with your personal files.

Go ahead and run `reset_apn_settings` now, and let me know when it's done. Then we'll do the reboot!

APN settings will reset at reboot.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Okay, I did the **reset APN settings**. It says **“APN settings will reset at reboot.”** My status bar still shows **excellent signal, 5G, and data enabled**.

So I guess the next step is the **reboot**, right? Just checking—restarting won’t delete anything?

You're doing everything perfectly! And yes, **the next step is the `reboot_device`**.

I promise—**rebooting will NOT delete anything**. It's just like turning your phone off and back on again. All your pictures, contacts, texts, and apps will be exactly the same when it comes back on. The only thing that will change is that the new APN settings will take effect, which should fix your MMS!

Go ahead and run `reboot_device` now. Once your phone turns back on, try sending a picture message and let me know how it goes!

Resetting APN settings...
Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app can send MMS messages.

Alright, I did the reboot. It came back on and I didn’t lose anything (thank goodness).

And I just checked: **my messaging app can send MMS messages now** — the picture message finally went through.

Thank you for sticking with me. ###STOP###

user_scenario.persona:
At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hi there… I’m having trouble with my phone. For the past few hours I haven’t been able to send picture messages (MMS) in my messaging app. Regular texts seem fine, but when I try to send a photo it just won’t go through.

I’m John Smith, and my number is 555-123-2002. I’m at home in the U.S. Can you help me get MMS working again?

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 15.1, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1003", "phone_number": "555-123-2003", "status": "Suspended", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1003", "data_used_gb": 0.0, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-06-30", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-10-05", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": "2025-02-01"}
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "15.1", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}

{"message":"Thanks, John—found your account and line 555-123-2002 is **Active**, so this looks like an MMS/data issue rather than a suspension.

One key thing I see: your line has used **15.1 GB** out of a **15.0 GB** monthly limit (cycle ends **2025-02-28**). When you’re over the data limit, **mobile data can stop**, and MMS (picture messages) typically won’t send even if regular SMS texts still work.

To get MMS working again, you have two options:

1) **Add a small amount of data (data refuel)**
- Your plan’s refuel price is **$2.00 per GB**
- You can add up to **2 GB**

2) **Change to a plan with more data** (if available)

If you want the fastest fix right now: tell me how much data you want to refuel (**0.5 GB, 1 GB, 2 GB**, etc.). I’ll confirm the total cost and apply it to line **555-123-2002**.

Also, quick check: can you confirm whether **mobile data works for web browsing when Wi‑Fi is turned off**? (That will confirm this is the data-cap issue.)"}
Wi-Fi is turned OFF.

Airplane Mode: ON
SIM Card Status: active
Cellular Connection: no_service
Cellular Signal: none
Cellular Network Type: none
Mobile Data Enabled: Yes
Data Roaming Enabled: No
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No

Oh… I’m a little nervous about buying things on the phone, but I do want the quickest fix, and I *don’t* want to change my plan.

You asked if mobile data works with Wi‑Fi off: my Wi‑Fi is **off**, and it looks like **Airplane Mode is ON** and the phone says **no service** (no signal at all). So I don’t think browsing would work right now.

Do you want me to turn **Airplane Mode off** first, or should we go ahead with the data refuel? I’m willing to add **2 GB** if that’s what it takes.

{"message":"Turn **Airplane Mode OFF first**—with it ON, your phone will show **No Service** and MMS/mobile data won’t work regardless of your data balance.

Please do this:
1) Turn **Airplane Mode OFF**.
2) Wait ~30–60 seconds for the phone to reconnect.
3) Confirm you see **signal bars** and a network type (like **4G/5G**) at the top.

Then tell me what you see (signal bars + 4G/5G or still “No Service”).

After that, if you still can’t send MMS, we can do the quickest fix without changing your plan: **refuel 2 GB** on your line **555-123-2002**.
- Cost: **2 GB × $2.00/GB = $4.00** (added to your bill)

If you want me to apply the 2 GB refuel, reply: **“Yes, add 2 GB for $4.00 to 555-123-2002.”**"}

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Okay, I turned **Airplane Mode OFF**.

Now at the top I see **one little signal bar** (looks pretty weak) and it says **2G**. Battery is about **80%** and it does say data is enabled. It’s not saying “No Service” anymore.

What should I do next—try sending the picture again, or do you still want to add the **2 GB for $4**? I’m worried I’ll press the wrong thing.

{"message":"Next step: **don’t buy/add data yet.** First we need to get you off **2G**, because **MMS usually won’t send on 2G** (it needs at least **3G**, and ideally 4G/5G).

Please do this in your phone’s cellular/mobile settings:
1) Find **Network mode / Preferred network type**.
2) Change it from anything like **2G only** to **“4G/5G preferred”** (or **LTE/5G**).
3) Wait 1–2 minutes and tell me what the status bar shows (does it change from **2G** to **4G/5G**? how many bars?).

After it’s on **3G/4G/5G**, try sending the picture again.

If you still can’t send MMS at that point, your line is also **over the monthly data limit** (15.1 GB used of 15.0 GB), and the quickest fix (without changing your plan) would be a **2 GB refuel for $4.00**. If you want me to do that, reply: **“Yes, add 2 GB for $4.00 to 555-123-2002.”**"}

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Alright, I did what you said. I changed it to **4G/5G preferred**, and now I see **5G** with **4 bars** at the top, so that looks much better.

But… I tried the picture message again and it **still won’t send**. This is getting a bit frustrating—I just want to send a photo of my grandkids.

Since you said I’m over my data limit and that can stop MMS, please go ahead:

**Yes, add 2 GB for $4.00 to 555-123-2002.**
{"message": "Successfully added 2 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

{"message":"I’ve added **2 GB** of data refuel to line **555-123-2002**.
- Charge: **$4.00**
- Refuel added: **2.0 GB**

Now please **reboot your phone** (power it off and back on). After it comes back up, try sending the picture message again.

If it still won’t send after the reboot, tell me what happens (does it say “failed”, “not sent”, stuck “sending”, etc.), and whether you’re still showing **5G** in the status bar."}

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Okay, I rebooted it (I was worried I’d lose my photos, but it seems everything is still here).

After it came back on, I’m still showing **5G** with **4 bars** and it says data is enabled.

But I tried sending the picture again and it still **won’t send**. It just sits there like it’s **“sending”** and then it doesn’t go through.

What do we do now?

[{"bill_id": "B1003", "customer_id": "C1001", "period_start": "2025-03-01", "period_end": "2025-03-31", "issue_date": "2025-03-01", "total_due": 4.0, "due_date": "2025-03-15", "line_items": [{"description": "Data refueling: 2 GB at $2.0/GB", "amount": 4.0, "date": "2025-02-25", "item_type": "Charge"}], "status": "Draft"}, {"bill_id": "B1002", "customer_id": "C1001", "period_start": "2025-02-01", "period_end": "2025-02-28", "issue_date": "2025-02-05", "total_due": 150.0, "due_date": "2025-02-19", "line_items": [{"description": "Basic Plan - Line 555-123-2001", "amount": 40.0, "date": "2025-02-05", "item_type": "Plan Charge"}, {"description": "Premium Plan - Line 555-123-2002", "amount": 65.0, "date": "2025-02-05", "item_type": "Plan Charge"}, {"description": "Basic Plan - Line 555-123-2003", "amount": 40.0, "date": "2025-02-05", "item_type": "Plan Charge"}, {"description": "Suspension Fee - Line 555-123-2003", "amount": 5.0, "date": "2025-02-05", "item_type": "Fee"}], "status": "Issued"}, {"bill_id": "B1001", "customer_id": "C1001", "period_start": "2025-01-01", "period_end": "2025-01-31", "issue_date": "2025-01-05", "total_due": 160.5, "due_date": "2025-01-19", "line_items": [{"description": "Basic Plan - Line 555-123-2001", "amount": 40.0, "date": "2025-01-05", "item_type": "Plan Charge"}, {"description": "Premium Plan - Line 555-123-2002", "amount": 65.0, "date": "2025-01-05", "item_type": "Plan Charge"}, {"description": "Basic Plan - Line 555-123-2003", "amount": 40.0, "date": "2025-01-05", "item_type": "Plan Charge"}, {"description": "Data Overage - Line 555-123-2002", "amount": 15.5, "date": "2025-01-05", "item_type": "Overage"}], "status": "Paid"}]

{"message":"Got it — since you’re now on **5G (4 bars)** and we’ve already added data + rebooted, the next most common MMS blocker is **APN/MMSC settings** or a feature that interferes with MMS (like **Wi‑Fi Calling**, **VPN**, or **Data Saver**).

Please try these steps in order (stop as soon as MMS sends):

1) **Confirm mobile data works at all**
- With Wi‑Fi still OFF, open a website (any simple page) to confirm cellular data is actually working.

2) **Turn OFF Wi‑Fi Calling (if it’s on)**
- Settings → Cellular/Mobile (or Phone) → **Wi‑Fi Calling** → set to **OFF**
- Then try sending the picture again.

3) **Make sure you’re not connected to a VPN**
- If you use a VPN app, disconnect it temporarily.
- Try sending the picture again.

4) **Reset APN settings (most effective for “stuck on sending” MMS)**
- Settings → Cellular/Mobile Network → **Access Point Names (APN)**
- Choose **Reset APN settings** / **Reset to default**
- Then **reboot the phone once more**
- After it’s back up, try sending the picture again.

Reply with:
- Whether web browsing worked on cellular data in step 1
- Whether Wi‑Fi Calling was ON or OFF
- And after the APN reset + reboot, does the MMS still get stuck on “sending” or does it fail with an error?"}
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.
Wi-Fi Calling is currently turned OFF.
VPN is turned OFF.

APN settings will reset at reboot.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Resetting APN settings...
Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app can send MMS messages.

Okay, I followed those steps as best as I could.

1) With Wi‑Fi off, I did a little internet check and it seems to be working — the speed test says **275 Mbps** (it said “excellent”).  
2) **Wi‑Fi Calling was already OFF.**  
3) **VPN is OFF** too (I don’t think I use one).  
4) I did the **Reset APN settings**, then rebooted again.

Now when I check, it says my messaging app **can send MMS messages**. I just tried sending the picture again and it finally went through.

Thank you… I was really worked up about this. ###STOP###

I’m at home in the United States, and my number is 555-123-2002. What do I need to do?
