{
  "message": "Thanks, John — I found your line (555-123-2002) and it’s Active, so this isn’t a suspension issue.

What I do see is that this line has used **15.1 GB** on a **15.0 GB** plan limit (Premium Plan). When you exceed the plan’s monthly data limit, **mobile data can be cut off**, and since **MMS (picture messages) requires mobile data**, photos will fail even if regular SMS texts still work.

To fix this right now, you have two options:
1) **Refuel data** (add up to **2 GB**). Your plan’s refuel rate is **$2.00 per GB**.
2) **Change to a plan with more data**.

If you want the fastest fix, tell me how much data you want to refuel: **1 GB ($2)** or **2 GB ($4)**, and I’ll apply it to 555-123-2002."
}

Oh dear… that would explain it, I guess. I really don’t want to change my plan, but I do need the picture messages to work again.

Please go ahead and **refuel 2 GB ($4)** on **555-123-2002**.

Will that bring my MMS back right away, and it won’t mess with my photos or anything on my phone, will it?
{"message": "Successfully added 2 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Okay… I did the reboot like you said (I was nervous about my photos, but it seems fine).

But it’s still not working — my phone is showing **Airplane Mode** at the top, and when I try it, my messaging app **still can’t send MMS**.

What should I do next?

{
  "message": "Airplane Mode being ON will block cellular service and mobile data, so MMS won’t send even with the extra data added.

Please do this next:
1) **Turn Airplane Mode OFF** (Settings → Network/Connections → Airplane Mode, or from Control Center/Quick Settings).
2) Wait ~30–60 seconds for signal to return.
3) Make sure **Mobile Data is ON**.
4) Try sending the picture message again.

If it still won’t send after Airplane Mode is OFF, tell me what you see at the top of the screen for:
- Signal (No Signal / bars)
- Network type (5G/4G/LTE/3G)
- Whether mobile data shows as enabled

And we’ll go to the next step."
}

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Alright, I turned **Airplane Mode OFF**.

At the top of my screen I now see: **poor signal (1 bar)**, it says **2G**, and it also shows **Data Enabled**. Battery is **80%**.

I tried again, and my messaging app **still can’t send MMS**. What now? I’m getting a bit frustrated—I really just need to send a photo to my daughter.

{
  "message": "2G is the issue here: **MMS generally won’t send reliably on 2G**. We need your phone to connect on **at least 3G (ideally 4G/5G)**.

Please do these steps in order:
1) Check your **network mode / preferred network type** and change it from anything like “2G” to **“4G/5G preferred”** (or **“4G only”** if that’s available).
2) After changing that setting, **reboot your phone**.
3) Once it comes back on, confirm the top of the screen no longer says **2G** (it should show 3G/4G/5G/LTE), then try sending the photo again.

If it still won’t send after that, do this next:
4) **Reset APN settings to default**, then **reboot** again and retry MMS.

Reply with what it shows after step 3 (network type + signal bars), and whether the MMS send works."
}
Network Mode Preference: 2g_only

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Okay, I did what you said:

- I checked that “preferred network” thing, and it was set to **2G only** (no wonder!).
- I changed it to **4G/5G preferred**.
- Now after the reboot, the top of my screen shows **4 bars**, **5G**, and **Data Enabled**.

But… I tried sending the picture again and my messaging app **still cannot send MMS**.

Do I really need to do that APN reset now? I don’t really understand what that is, and I’m worried I’ll break something.

{
  "message": "Since you now have **5G**, strong signal, and **Data Enabled**, the next most common cause of MMS failing is **APN/MMSC settings** (the carrier settings your phone uses specifically for mobile data + picture messaging).

### Do you need the APN reset?
Yes—**it’s the next appropriate step** for “MMS won’t send” when cellular data is otherwise working.
- An **APN reset restores the default carrier settings** on your phone.
- It **won’t delete or change your photos** or other personal data.
- After the reset, you should **reboot** so the new settings apply.

### Before the APN reset (quick check)
1) If **Wi‑Fi Calling** is ON, turn it **OFF**, then try sending the MMS again.

### If it still won’t send: Reset APN
2) **Reset APN settings to default** on your phone.
3) **Reboot** your phone.
4) Try sending the picture message again.

Reply with two things after you do that:
- Whether MMS sends successfully
- Whether you can load a website using **mobile data** (with Wi‑Fi turned off temporarily)

If it still fails after the APN reset, we’ll check messaging permissions next (storage/SMS) and any remaining MMS configuration issues."
}
Wi-Fi Calling is currently turned OFF.

APN settings will reset at reboot.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Resetting APN settings...
Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app can send MMS messages.

Wi-Fi is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Okay, I checked the Wi‑Fi Calling first, and it was already **OFF**.

Then I went ahead and did the **APN reset**, and rebooted like you said (I was really nervous, but my pictures all seem to still be here).

Now when I try it, it says my messaging app **can send MMS messages** again. So I think that fixed it!

You also asked about loading a website using mobile data with Wi‑Fi off… do you want me to turn Wi‑Fi off and check that too?

No, that’s all I needed. I’m just relieved the picture messages are working again, and thank you for explaining that it wouldn’t touch my photos.

I’ll leave the website test alone since you said it’s optional.

Thanks again. ###STOP###

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hi… I’m hoping you can help me. I’m John Smith, and for the past few hours I haven’t been able to send picture messages (MMS) from my regular messaging app. Regular texts seem fine, but when I try to send a photo it just won’t go through.  

My phone number is 555-123-2002, and I’m at home here in the United States. What should I do?

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}

Thanks, John—I found your account for **555-123-2002** and we can troubleshoot MMS.

Since **SMS works but MMS won’t send**, the most common causes are: mobile data not working/disabled, incorrect APN (MMSC) settings, Wi‑Fi Calling interference, or the phone being stuck on an unsuitable network mode.

Please do these checks on your phone and tell me what you see:

1) **Check cellular + data status**
- Run **check_network_status()**
  - Tell me: **signal strength**, **network type (5G/4G/3G/2G)**, whether **Mobile Data is enabled**, and whether **Data Roaming is enabled**.

2) **Check MMS capability**
- Run **can_send_mms()**
  - Tell me what it reports.

3) **Check APN/MMSC**
- Run **check_apn_settings()**
  - Tell me the **APN name** and especially whether an **MMSC URL** is present.

If you want to try one quick fix immediately while you gather those results: **toggle Airplane Mode ON for 10 seconds, then OFF** (this forces a network re-register), then try sending the picture again and let me know if it changes anything.

Cellular Network Type: none

MMSC URL (for picture messages): Not Set
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)

Okay, I ran those checks you asked for. Here’s what they show:

1) **Network status (check_network_status)**
- **Airplane Mode:** ON  
- **SIM:** active  
- **Cellular connection:** **no service** (signal: **none**)  
- **Network type:** none  
- **Mobile Data:** Yes (enabled)  
- **Data Roaming:** No  
- **Wi‑Fi:** OFF / not connected  

2) **MMS capability (can_send_mms)**
- It says **my messaging app cannot send MMS messages**.

3) **APN/MMSC (check_apn_settings)**
- **APN name:** internet  
- **MMSC URL:** **Not Set**

One thing—Airplane Mode being **ON** surprised me. If I turn that off, am I going to lose anything like my photos? And do you want me to toggle Airplane Mode now (since it’s already on)?

{
  "message": "Turning **Airplane Mode OFF will not delete or affect your photos (or any data)**—it only turns your phone’s wireless radios (cellular/Wi‑Fi/Bluetooth) on or off.

Right now, Airplane Mode being **ON** is why you have **No Service**, and MMS won’t work until cellular service is restored.

Please do this in order:

1) **Turn Airplane Mode OFF**
- Use **toggle_airplane_mode(OFF)**.

2) After it’s OFF, wait ~30 seconds and run **check_network_status()** again
- Tell me what it shows for **signal strength** and **network type (5G/4G/3G/etc.)**.

3) Then, because your **MMSC URL is Not Set**, we’ll fix MMS settings:
- Run **reset_apn_settings()**
- Then run **reboot_device()**

After the reboot, try sending a picture message again.

To your question about “toggling” Airplane Mode: since it’s already ON, the key step is switching it **OFF** first. If things still look stuck afterward, we can do a quick ON→OFF toggle again later."
}

Airplane Mode is now OFF.

Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No

Okay, I turned **Airplane Mode OFF** (and thank you for reassuring me about the photos).

After waiting a bit, I ran **check_network_status()** again. Now it shows:

- **Airplane Mode:** OFF  
- **Cellular connection:** connected  
- **Signal:** **poor**  
- **Network type:** **2G**  
- **Mobile Data:** enabled (Yes)  
- **Data roaming:** No  
- **Wi‑Fi:** Off / not connected  

You listed two more steps (**reset_apn_settings()** and then **reboot_device()**). I can do them, but I’m nervous about rebooting—will rebooting definitely not erase anything? And do you want me to do **reset APN** first, or the **reboot** first?

{
  "message": "Rebooting is safe—**it will not erase your photos, messages, or apps**. A reboot just restarts the phone’s software (similar to restarting a computer). The only thing you might lose is something unsaved that’s currently open (e.g., an unsent draft in an app), so it’s fine to close any apps first if you want.

And yes: **do Reset APN first, then reboot** (the reboot helps the APN/MMSC changes fully apply).

Please do these steps in this order:

1) **reset_apn_settings()**
2) **reboot_device()**

After the phone comes back up:

3) Run **check_network_status()** again
- If it still shows **Network type: 2G**, MMS may still fail because **2G isn’t suitable for MMS**. In that case, change your network mode preference to a mode that allows 4G/5G (e.g., **set_network_mode_preference("4g_5g_preferred")**), then re-check network status.

4) Try sending a picture message again, and also run **can_send_mms()** and tell me the result."
}

APN settings will reset at reboot.

So it looks like it’s working again. Thank you—this stuff really stresses me out. ###STOP###

user_scenario.persona:
At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hi there—I'm hoping you can help me. I haven’t been able to send picture messages (MMS) from my messaging app for the past few hours. Regular texts seem fine, but any photo message just won’t go through.

Can you help me get it working again?

To help with MMS troubleshooting, I first need to identify your account.

Please reply with **one** of the following:
- **Your phone number** (the line having the MMS issue), or
- **Customer ID**, or
- **Full name + date of birth (YYYY-MM-DD)**

Also, tell me:
1) Are you on **iPhone or Android**?  
2) Do picture messages **fail on cellular data, Wi‑Fi, or both**?  
3) What does it say when it fails (e.g., “Not delivered,” “Message failed,” stuck “Sending”)?
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%

Airplane Mode: ON
SIM Card Status: active
Cellular Connection: no_service
Cellular Signal: none
Cellular Network Type: none
Mobile Data Enabled: Yes
Data Roaming Enabled: No
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

My phone number is **555-123-2002** (John Smith).

1) I’m not sure if it’s iPhone or Android — I get a little mixed up with that stuff.  
2) I also don’t really know about cellular data vs Wi‑Fi, but I can tell you right now my phone shows **Airplane Mode is ON** and **Wi‑Fi is OFF**.  
3) When I try, it just won’t send — it fails, and the phone **can’t send MMS** at the moment.

Is Airplane Mode being on the reason? If I turn that off, I won’t lose any photos, right?

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 15.1, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1003", "phone_number": "555-123-2003", "status": "Suspended", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1003", "data_used_gb": 0.0, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-06-30", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-10-05", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": "2025-02-01"}

Yes—**Airplane Mode being ON is very likely the reason** your MMS (picture messages) won’t send.

- **MMS requires a cellular connection (and mobile data).**  
- When **Airplane Mode is ON**, your phone turns off the cellular radio, so MMS will fail.
- Turning Airplane Mode OFF **will not delete or erase any photos** on your phone. It only changes wireless connection settings.

Your line **555-123-2002** is **Active** on our side, so nothing looks blocked at the account/line level.

### What to do now
1) **Turn Airplane Mode OFF** on your phone.  
2) Wait about **30–60 seconds** for signal to come back.  
3) Try sending a picture message again.

If it still fails after that, tell me what your phone shows in the status bar (signal bars / 4G/5G, etc.), and whether **Mobile Data** is enabled.

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Okay, I turned **Airplane Mode OFF**. I’m a bit nervous messing with settings, but it looks okay.

Now my status bar shows **1 bar (poor signal)** and it says **2G**, and it also shows **Data Enabled**. Battery is 80%.

I tried sending the picture message again and it **still can’t send MMS**. This is getting a little frustrating—I really need to send a photo of my grandkids. What should I do next?
{"line_id": "L1002", "data_used_gb": "15.1", "data_limit_gb": "15.0", "data_refueling_gb": "0.0", "cycle_end_date": "2025-02-28"}
{"plan_id": "P1002", "name": "Premium Plan", "data_limit_gb": 15.0, "price_per_month": 65.0, "data_refueling_price_per_gb": 2.0}

Your phone showing **2G** plus your line being **over the monthly data limit** explains this.

- MMS needs **mobile data**, and **2G is often not sufficient for MMS**.
- On our side, line **555-123-2002** has used **15.1 GB** on a **15.0 GB** plan (cycle ends **2025-02-28**). When you exceed the limit, data (and MMS) can stop working.

### Next steps (fastest fix)
You have two options:

1) **Refuel data** (add up to **2 GB**)  
   - Your plan’s refuel rate is **$2.00 per GB**.
   - Tell me how much you want to add: **0.5 GB, 1 GB, 1.5 GB, or 2 GB**, and I can apply it to your line.

2) **Change to a higher-data plan** (if available)  
   - I can pull the available plans and you can pick one.

Also, since your phone is currently on **2G**, once data is available again you may need to switch your phone’s network preference to **4G/5G preferred** (I can guide you through that after we restore data).

How much data would you like to **refuel** (up to 2 GB), or would you rather **change plans**?

Oh—so I’ve gone over my data limit. I didn’t realize that would stop picture messages.

I **don’t want to change my plan**, but I am willing to do the **refuel** if that’s the quickest way to get my picture message working again.

Please add **2.0 GB** to **555-123-2002**. What do you need from me to pay the **$4.00**?
{"message": "Successfully added 2.0 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

I’ve added **2.0 GB** of data refuel to **555-123-2002**. The charge is **$4.00**, and it’s been applied to your account.

### What to do next (to get MMS sending)
1) **Restart (reboot) your phone**.
2) After it turns back on, try sending the picture message again.
3) If it still won’t send, check whether your phone is still stuck on **2G**—if it is, we’ll need to switch your network mode to **4G/5G preferred** (I’ll walk you through it).

If you tell me whether you see **2G / 4G / 5G** after the reboot, I’ll guide the next step.

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Okay, I went ahead and **restarted the phone**. I was worried I’d lose pictures, but everything still seems to be there.

After the reboot, my status bar still shows **1 bar (poor)** and it still says **2G**, with **Data Enabled**.

I tried sending the picture message again and it **still cannot send MMS**.

I’m getting a bit discouraged—what do we do to switch it to that **4G/5G preferred** thing you mentioned?

{"device_id": "D1002", "device_type": "phone", "model": "Smartphone Pro Max", "imei": "234567890123456", "is_esim_capable": true, "activated": true, "activation_date": "2025-02-15 11:45:00", "last_esim_transfer_date": "2025-01-20 09:30:00"}

{
  "message": "Let’s do this next: your phone is stuck on **2G**, and **2G often won’t support MMS**, even if data is enabled and you refueled data.

### Step 1 — Switch your phone’s Network Mode to 4G/5G preferred
On your phone, find the setting usually called **Network mode**, **Preferred network type**, or **Mobile network** (you can often use the Settings search bar), then set it to:
- **4G/5G preferred** (sometimes shown as **LTE/5G** or **Auto (5G/4G/3G)**)

If you only see options like **2G/3G**, choose the highest available option.

### Step 2 — Reconnect and retest
After changing the network mode:
1) Wait **30–60 seconds**
2) Confirm the status bar no longer says **2G** (ideally it shows **4G/LTE** or **5G**)
3) Try sending the picture message again

### If it still shows 2G after changing the mode
That usually means **there isn’t 4G/5G coverage where you are right now** (or it can’t lock onto it with that weak signal). If that happens, tell me:
- After changing the setting, what options did you actually see (exact wording)?
- Does it still show **2G**, or did it change to **3G/4G/5G**?

Reply with what you see and I’ll guide the next step."
}

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Your messaging app cannot send MMS messages.

Alright, I switched the network mode to **4G/5G preferred** (I’m hoping I did that right).

Now the status bar looks much better: it shows **4 bars (excellent)** and **5G**, and still **Data Enabled**.

But I tried sending the picture message again and it **still can’t send MMS**. What else can we check?

{
  "message": "Thanks — getting to **5G with strong signal** rules out coverage and network-mode issues. Next we’ll check the two most common MMS blockers: **APN/MMSC settings** and **Wi‑Fi Calling / app permissions**.

### 1) Confirm mobile data is actually working
With **Wi‑Fi OFF**, open a web page (any site) in your browser.
- If browsing **doesn’t** work, tell me (that would point to a data connectivity/APN issue).

### 2) Turn OFF Wi‑Fi Calling (it can interfere with MMS)
Go into Settings and find **Wi‑Fi Calling** and switch it **OFF**, then try sending the picture message again.

### 3) Reset APN settings to default (fixes missing/incorrect MMSC)
In your cellular/mobile network settings, find **APN** settings and choose **Reset APN settings** (or “Reset to default”).
Then **reboot your phone once more**, and try MMS again.

### 4) Check messaging app permissions (if you’re on Android)
Make sure your Messaging app has permissions for **SMS** and **Storage**. If either is off, turn it on and retry.

Reply with:
1) Whether web browsing works on mobile data right now (Wi‑Fi off)
2) Whether **Wi‑Fi Calling** was ON or OFF
3) After APN reset + reboot, does MMS still fail?

Also, to tailor the steps: do you see an **App Store** (iPhone) or **Play Store** (Android) on your phone?"
}
Wi-Fi is turned OFF.
Wi-Fi Calling is currently turned OFF.

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): Not Set
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)
The following apps are installed on the phone: messaging, browser

APN settings will reset at reboot.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Resetting APN settings...
Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)
Your messaging app can send MMS messages.

I’m honestly a little overwhelmed by all these settings, but I did what you said as best I could.

Here’s what I can tell you:

1) **Wi‑Fi is OFF**, and my phone shows **5G with good signal** and **Data Enabled**.  
2) **Wi‑Fi Calling was already OFF.**  
3) We **reset the APN settings** and then **rebooted** the phone. After that, the picture messaging setting (I think you called it the MMSC?) seems to be filled in now.

And the good news: **it can send MMS messages now** — my picture message finally went through.

Thank you. ###STOP###

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?
Hi—I'm John Smith. My phone has been showing **“No Service”** for the past few hours, and I can’t make calls or use data. My number is **555-123-2002**. Can you help me get service back?
I'd be happy to help you get your service back, John. Let me look up your account first using your phone number.

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}

I found your account, John. I can see you have multiple lines on your account. Let me check the details of the line associated with your phone number (555-123-2002) to understand what's causing the "No Service" issue.

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 8.7, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}

Good news - I can see that your line (555-123-2002) shows as **Active** in our system, so there's no suspension or account issue causing this. The problem appears to be on your device's side rather than a carrier-level issue.

Let's troubleshoot your phone to restore your service. Please start by checking your phone's status bar:

1. **Can you check your status bar** at the top of your phone screen and tell me what icons you see? Specifically:
   - Do you see an airplane icon (✈️)?
   - What does your signal indicator show (any bars, or just "No Service")?

Also, could you **check your SIM card status** in your phone settings? This will help me understand if the SIM card is being detected properly.
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%
The SIM card is locked with a PIN code.

I checked.

- Status bar: it shows **“No Signal”** (no bars). I **don’t** see an airplane icon. It also shows **Data Disabled** and battery **80%**.
- SIM status: it says the **SIM card is locked with a PIN code**.

What should I do next?

Thank you for checking that information. I've identified the issue:

**Your SIM card is locked with a PIN code.** This is preventing your phone from connecting to the cellular network, which explains the "No Signal" status.

Unfortunately, SIM card security issues (PIN/PUK locks) require specialized assistance that I cannot provide directly. I'll need to transfer you to a human agent who can help you unlock your SIM card securely.

Customer needs assistance with SIM card PIN/PUK unlock to restore service.

YOU ARE BEING TRANSFERRED TO A HUMAN AGENT. PLEASE HOLD ON.

Hi—I'm actually the customer. My phone has been showing **“No Service”** for the past few hours, and I can’t get a signal. Can you help me troubleshoot?

To troubleshoot “No Service,” I first need to identify your account.

Please reply with **one** of the following:
- **The phone number** that’s showing “No Service,” or
- **Your customer ID**, or
- **Your full name + date of birth (YYYY-MM-DD)**

Once I have that, I’ll check your line status (active/suspended) and then walk you through device steps (Airplane Mode, SIM status, network settings).
The phone number is **555-123-2002**.

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 8.7, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1003", "phone_number": "555-123-2003", "status": "Suspended", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1003", "data_used_gb": 0.0, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-06-30", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-10-05", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": "2025-02-01"}

I looked up your line **555-123-2002** on the account and it shows **Status: Active** (not suspended) and the contract end date is in the future—so this looks like a **device/network connection issue** rather than an account block.

Please try these in order and tell me what you see after each step:

1) **Toggle Airplane Mode**
- Turn **Airplane Mode ON** for 10 seconds, then **OFF**.
- Check if signal returns.

2) **Reboot the phone**
- Power the phone completely off, wait 15 seconds, then turn it back on.

3) **Check SIM status**
- Does your phone show anything like **“SIM missing” / “SIM not provisioned” / “SIM locked”**?
- If you have a **physical SIM**, remove it and reinsert it (SIM reseat), then restart once more.

4) **Check network settings**
- Make sure **Cellular/Mobile Data line is enabled**.
- Make sure **Network Selection = Automatic** (not manually locked to a carrier).
- Make sure the preferred network mode isn’t set to **2G/3G only** (set it to **5G/4G preferred** if available).

Reply with:
- Your **phone model** (iPhone or Android + model), and
- What your status bar shows right now (e.g., **“No Service”**, any signal bars, **SOS**, **E/3G/4G/5G**).

Airplane Mode is now ON.
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

I toggled Airplane Mode ON for ~10 seconds and back OFF.  

Right after turning it off, the status bar still shows: **No Signal** (and it also shows **Data Disabled**), battery **80%**.

Before I do the next step: do you want me to **reboot the phone** now? Also, I can’t confirm my phone model without checking—how should I check that on the device?

Yes—please **reboot the phone now**.

After it comes back up, please tell me exactly what the status bar shows (still **No Signal**, any bars, **SOS**, etc.). Also confirm that **Airplane Mode is OFF** after the reboot.

About checking your phone model: you can do it right on the device by opening **Settings** and looking for an **“About”** section (often labeled **About / About phone / General → About**). Then read me the **Model** (and if it shows it, whether it’s using **SIM** or **eSIM**).

One quick note: your status bar also shows **Data Disabled**. That won’t usually cause “No Signal,” but after we get signal back we can also turn **Mobile Data ON** if needed.

If the reboot doesn’t restore signal, the next step will be checking **SIM status** (whether it’s missing/locked) and then reseating the SIM if it’s a physical SIM.

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Okay, I rebooted the phone. After it came back up, the status bar still shows: **No Signal** and **Data Disabled** (battery **80%**).

Also, I don’t see the airplane icon, so **Airplane Mode looks OFF**.

This is getting a bit frustrating—nothing’s changed so far. What do you want me to try next: **check SIM status / reseat the SIM**?

{
  "message": "Yes—next step is to check **SIM status**, and then **reseat the SIM** if it’s a physical SIM.

## 1) Check SIM status (don’t remove anything yet)
On your phone, go to **Settings** and look for either:
- **About / About phone** → then look for **SIM status**, or
- **Cellular / Mobile Network / SIMs** → then look for the SIM/line status

Please tell me what it says (examples: **Active/Ready**, **No SIM/SIM missing**, **SIM locked (PIN/PUK)**, **Emergency calls only**, etc.).

## 2) If it says “No SIM / SIM missing” (physical SIM)
1. Power the phone **OFF**.
2. Remove the SIM tray, take the SIM out, and **reinsert it firmly**.
3. Power the phone **ON** and check if signal returns.

## Important
- If you see **SIM Locked / PIN / PUK**, **stop there and tell me**—that requires SIM security help.
- If it shows **Active/Ready** but still **No Signal**, we’ll move next to checking the **preferred network mode** (set to **4G/5G preferred**) and automatic network selection.

Reply with:
1) What the SIM status page says, and 2) whether you have a **physical SIM** (SIM tray) or **eSIM**."
}
The SIM card is locked with a PIN code.

I checked SIM status, and it says the **SIM card is locked with a PIN code**.

What should I do next? (You mentioned to stop here if it’s SIM Locked/PIN/PUK.)

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

I'm sorry to hear you're having trouble with your phone's service! I'd be happy to help you troubleshoot this issue step by step. Don't worry—I'll guide you through everything carefully.

First, I'll need to look up your account so I can check on your line's status. Could you please provide me with one of the following:

1. Your phone number (the one associated with the line having issues)
2. Your customer ID
3. Or your full name and date of birth

Once I have your account information, we can start figuring out what's causing the "No Service" issue.

Sure… my name is **John Smith**, and the phone number is **555-123-2002**.

Do you need my date of birth too? I’m a little nervous about giving out too much, but I really do need the phone working.

I completely understand your concern about privacy—that's very sensible! However, I do need your date of birth to verify your identity and access your account. This is a security measure to protect your information and ensure I'm speaking with the right person.

Rest assured, this information is only used for verification purposes. Could you please provide your date of birth so I can look up your account and help get your phone working again?

I understand, but I actually don’t have my date of birth handy right now, and I get a bit uneasy sharing it.

Is there another way you can verify me—like sending a code to my email, or using the phone number and my name only?

I completely understand your hesitation, and I appreciate you being cautious with your personal information.

The good news is that I can actually look up your account using just your phone number! Let me try that now.

I found the issue, John. The line associated with your phone number (555-123-2002) is currently **Suspended**, which explains why you're seeing "No Service."

I can see the suspension started today (February 25th). Let me check your bills to see if there's an overdue balance that may have caused this suspension.

I found the cause of the suspension. You have an **overdue bill** (Bill ID: B1234321) for **$65.00** that was due on January 15th, 2025. This unpaid bill is why your line has been suspended.

Here's what we can do to get your service restored:

1. **Pay the overdue bill** of $65.00
2. Once paid, I can **lift the suspension** on your line
3. After that, you'll just need to **reboot your phone** to restore service

Would you like me to send you a payment request for this $65.00 overdue bill so we can get your phone working again?

Yes… if that’s what’s causing it, please go ahead and **send me the payment request for the $65.00** so I can pay it and get my service back.

And just to ask (sorry, I get anxious about this): when you say “reboot” afterward, that won’t delete my photos, will it?

Great question, and no need to apologize at all! **Rebooting your phone will NOT delete your photos, contacts, or any of your data.** A reboot is simply turning your phone off and back on again—it just refreshes the phone's systems and connections. All your personal files stay safe. 😊

Now let me send that payment request for you:

2. **Accept and complete the payment**
3. **Let me know once you've paid**, and I'll lift the suspension on your line
4. After that, just **reboot your phone** (turn it off, wait a few seconds, turn it back on) to restore your service

What do I do next—can you lift the suspension now?
Let me verify that the payment went through, and then I'll lift the suspension for you right away.

Excellent news, John! 🎉 Your line has been **successfully reactivated**! Here's the final step:

**Reboot your phone now:**
1. Press and hold the power button on your phone
2. Select "Restart" or "Power Off"
3. If you powered it off, wait a few seconds, then turn it back on

Once your phone restarts, you should see your signal bars return and be able to make calls and send texts again.

Let me know if you have any trouble after rebooting, and I'll be happy to help further!

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔋 80%

I restarted the phone, but now I’m seeing an **airplane** symbol up at the top — it says **Airplane Mode**, and the battery is about **80%**. I still don’t see any signal bars.

Did I accidentally turn something on? What should I do next?

No worries, John! You didn't break anything—this is an easy fix. **Airplane Mode** is a setting that turns off all wireless connections (cellular, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth), which is why you're not seeing any signal bars.

It's very easy to accidentally turn on, so don't worry! Let's turn it off:

**To turn off Airplane Mode:**
1. Swipe down from the top of your screen to open your quick settings
2. Look for the **airplane icon** and tap it to turn it **OFF**
3. Wait a few seconds for your phone to reconnect to the network

Alternatively, you can:
1. Go to **Settings**
2. Look for **Airplane Mode** (or "Flight Mode")
3. Toggle it **OFF**

Once you turn it off, you should see your signal bars come back within a few seconds. Let me know how it goes!

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Okay, I turned **Airplane Mode off** like you said. Thank you—at least that little airplane is gone.

But I’m still seeing **“No Signal”**, and it also says **Data Disabled**, battery **80%**. This is getting a little frustrating… I thought paying the bill would fix it.

What should I do now?

I completely understand your frustration, John—but don't worry, we're making progress! The good news is that Airplane Mode is off now, and your line is active on our end.

The **"Data Disabled"** message tells me your mobile data is simply turned off, which might also be affecting your overall connectivity. Let's turn that on and check a couple more things.

**To turn on Mobile Data:**
1. Swipe down from the top of your screen to open your quick settings
2. Look for a **"Mobile Data"** or **"Data"** icon and tap it to turn it **ON**
3. Or go to **Settings** > **Network & Internet** (or "Connections") > **Mobile Data** and toggle it **ON**

After you do that, let me know what you see in the status bar. Specifically:
- Do you see any signal bars now?
- Does it still say "No Signal" or do you see something like "4G" or "5G"?

We'll get this sorted out—you're doing great!

Mobile Data is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Airplane Mode: OFF
SIM Card Status: missing
Cellular Connection: no_service
Cellular Signal: none
Cellular Network Type: none
Mobile Data Enabled: Yes
Data Roaming Enabled: No
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No

Alright, I tried turning **Mobile Data** on. I might have hit it twice by mistake, but it’s showing as **Mobile Data Enabled: Yes** when I check.

Right now my status bar still shows **“No Signal”** and **“Data Disabled”** with **80%** battery.

I also checked the network status like you asked, and it says:

- **Airplane Mode: OFF**
- **SIM Card Status: missing**
- **Cellular Connection: no_service**
- **Cellular Signal: none**
- **Mobile Data Enabled: Yes**
- **Wi‑Fi: OFF / not connected**

What does “SIM missing” mean—did something fall out of the phone?

Great job checking all that, John! You've actually found the problem—**"SIM Card Status: missing"** is the issue.

The SIM card is a small chip inside your phone that connects you to our network. Sometimes it can become slightly loose or not seated properly, especially if the phone has been bumped or dropped. It doesn't necessarily mean it fell out—it might just need to be reseated (taken out and put back in).

**Here's what I'd like you to try:**

1. **Find the SIM card tray** on your phone—it's usually on the side of the phone and has a tiny pinhole next to it
2. Use a SIM ejector tool (a small pin that may have come with your phone) or a paperclip to gently push into the pinhole
3. The tray will pop out slightly—carefully pull it out
4. You should see a small chip (the SIM card) sitting in the tray
5. Make sure it's positioned correctly and flat in the tray
6. Slide the tray back in until it clicks

**Don't worry**—this won't affect your photos or data at all. You're just making sure the SIM card is properly connected.

After you reseat the SIM card, let me know what your status bar shows. Does that sound okay to try?

SIM card re-seated successfully.
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%
The SIM card is locked with a PIN code.
Status Bar: 📵 No Signal | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔋 80%

Okay, I did the SIM thing (I’m always afraid I’ll snap something, but I managed). It says the SIM was re-seated successfully.

But I’m still seeing **No Signal** on the top.

When I checked the SIM status, it now says: **“The SIM card is locked with a PIN code.”**

I don’t remember ever setting a PIN… what am I supposed to do here? I’m worried I’ll lock myself out.

Great job reseating the SIM card, John! You did that perfectly—the phone now recognizes it.

The **"SIM card is locked with a PIN code"** message means the SIM has a security PIN that needs to be entered to unlock it. Sometimes these are set by default or were set up when the account was created.

**Important:** I want to be careful here because if the wrong PIN is entered too many times, the SIM can become locked with a PUK code, which is more complicated to resolve.

**Do not guess the PIN** if you don't remember it.

Since you don't recall setting a PIN, I'm going to **transfer you to a human agent** who specializes in SIM security. They'll be able to safely help you unlock your SIM card without risking it getting locked further.

Let me transfer you now.

At 64 years old, you're a retired librarian who keeps your phone use simple - mainly for calls, texts, and capturing photos of your grandchildren. Technology in general makes you feel uneasy and overwhelmed.

Your technical knowledge is quite limited. Step-by-step instructions often confuse you, and technical terms like "VPN" or "APN" might as well be a foreign language. You only share information when specifically asked.

When dealing with technology, you tend to get flustered quickly. You need constant reassurance and often interrupt with anxious questions. Simple requests like "reboot the phone" can trigger worries about losing precious photos.

- **Data Limit**: 15.0 GB
- **Billing Cycle Ends**: February 28, 2025

Since you've used all 15 GB of your monthly data allowance, your data connectivity has been cut off. This is why you're experiencing extremely slow or no data service.

You have two options to restore your mobile data:

1. **Refuel Data**: Add more data to your line at **$2.00 per GB** (up to 2 GB max). For example:
   - 1 GB = $2.00
   - 2 GB = $4.00

2. **Change to a plan with more data**: You could upgrade to a different plan with a higher data limit.

Since you're traveling and need immediate connectivity, I'd recommend refueling data. Would you like me to add some data to your line? If so, how many GB would you like (up to 2 GB)?

That's correct! **2 GB at $2.00/GB = $4.00 total**, which will be added to your next bill.

Let me process that refuel for you now.
{"message": "Successfully added 2.0 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

Your data has been refueled. Here's a summary:

Your mobile data should now be working again in France. If it doesn't reconnect automatically within a few minutes, try these quick steps on your phone:

1. **Toggle Airplane Mode**: Turn it ON, wait a few seconds, then turn it OFF again. This will force your phone to reconnect to the network.

2. **Make sure Data Roaming is enabled on your device**: Go to your phone's settings and ensure Data Roaming is turned ON (this is separate from the roaming we enable on our end).

Is there anything else I can help you with?

Airplane Mode is now OFF.

Right after toggling, my status bar shows: **poor signal (1 bar), 2G, “Data Disabled,” VPN connected, battery 80%**. So it looks like my mobile data is actually turned off on the phone right now.

Thank you for that information! I can see a few issues from your status bar that we need to address. Let's tackle them one at a time, starting with the most critical:

**Step 1: Turn Mobile Data ON**

Since your status bar shows "Data Disabled," your mobile data is turned off on your device. Please go to your phone's settings and **toggle mobile data ON**.

Once you've done that, let me know what your status bar shows and whether your data is working. We may need to address a couple more things I noticed:

- You're connected to a **2G network**, which is very slow and may not provide adequate data speeds
- You have a **VPN connected**, which can also slow down your connection

But first, let's get mobile data turned on. Let me know once you've done that!

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%

Okay, I turned **mobile data ON**.

Now my status bar shows: **poor signal (1 bar), 2G, Data Enabled, Data Saver is on, VPN connected, battery 80%**.

Data still feels basically unusable. What should I do next?

Good progress! I can see several things that are likely causing your data to be unusable. Let's fix them one by one:

**Step 2: Turn OFF Data Saver mode**

Data Saver restricts background data and can make your connection feel very slow. Please go to your settings and **toggle Data Saver OFF**.

**Step 3: Disconnect the VPN**

VPNs can significantly slow down your connection, especially when roaming. Please **disconnect your VPN**.

**Step 4: Change your Network Mode Preference**

You're currently on a **2G network**, which is far too slow for usable data. Please go to your network settings and **change your network mode preference to "4G/5G Preferred"**. This will allow your phone to connect to faster networks.

Please complete these three steps and let me know what your status bar shows afterward, and whether your data connection has improved!

I can do those, but I can only do **one step at a time** on my end here.

Which should I do first: **turn off Data Saver**, **disconnect the VPN**, or **change network mode to 4G/5G preferred**?

Great question! **Change your network mode to "4G/5G Preferred" first.**

Being stuck on 2G is the biggest issue — 2G is essentially unusable for data in most cases. Switching to 4G/5G will have the most significant impact on your connection speed.

Once you've done that, let me know what your status bar shows!

I switched the network mode to **4G/5G Preferred**.

Now the status bar shows: **excellent signal (4 bars), 5G, Data Enabled, Data Saver on, VPN connected, battery 80%**.

But when I tried a speed test, it came back **“Speed test failed: No Connection.”** So it still isn’t actually connecting, even though it looks better. What should I do next?

That's interesting — you have excellent 5G signal and data is enabled, but still no connection. Since you're traveling in France, I suspect the issue is that **Data Roaming is turned OFF on your device**.

Even though roaming is enabled on your line from our side, your phone also has its own Data Roaming setting that needs to be turned ON to allow data usage outside your home network.

**Next step: Turn ON Data Roaming**

Please go to your phone's settings and **toggle Data Roaming ON**.

Let me know what happens after that!

I turned **Data Roaming ON**. Status bar still shows: **excellent 5G, Data Enabled, Data Saver on, VPN connected**.

I ran a speed test and it’s **5.50 Mbps (Fair)**.

I’m a bit frustrated—this still isn’t what I need. I need **excellent** speed, not fair. What should we try next?

Great news — you have a working connection now! But I understand you need better speeds. You still have two things that can slow down your connection:

1. **Data Saver is ON** — restricts data usage and can limit speeds
2. **VPN is connected** — VPNs can significantly slow down your connection, especially when roaming internationally

**Next step: Disconnect the VPN**

The VPN is likely the bigger culprit here. When you're roaming, your data has to travel through the VPN server (possibly in another country), which adds extra distance and slows things down considerably.

Please **disconnect your VPN** and then run another speed test. Let me know the result!

VPN disconnected successfully.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 55.00 Mbps (Good). Connection is good for most activities, including HD streaming.

I disconnected the VPN. Now the status bar shows: **excellent 5G, Data Enabled, Data Saver still on**.

Speed test is now **55.00 Mbps (Good)**.

That’s better, but I’m still not calling it fixed—I need **excellent** speed. What next, turn off **Data Saver**?

Exactly! **Turn OFF Data Saver** next.

Data Saver mode restricts data usage and can limit your connection speed. Turning it off should give you that extra boost to reach excellent speeds.

Please **toggle Data Saver OFF** and run another speed test. Let me know what you get!

Data Saver Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.

I turned **Data Saver OFF**.

Status bar now: **excellent 5G, Data Enabled, battery 80%**.

I re-ran the speed test and got **275.00 Mbps (Excellent)**. That’s exactly what I needed — looks like it’s fixed now. ###STOP###

The user is experiencing issues with their mobile data. They are unable to use their phone to browse the internet, and the status bar shows 'No Service'. Customer name: John Smith, phone number: 555-123-2002, current location: abroad in France. They will consider the issue resolved when speed test returns excellent internet speed. They will not change their mobile data plan but they will refuel 2.0 GB of data if necessary.

You mobile data is not working properly. It either stops working or is very slow. You want to fix it and absolutely want to get excellent internet speed on your phone. You are not willing to accept any other internet speed (poor, fair or good). You do not have access to wifi.

If the agent suggests actions that don't immediately fix the issue, follow their guidance but express mild frustration after the first unsuccessful attempt. You will consider the issue resolved only when speed test returns excellent internet speed and nothing else. If it returns poor, fair or good, you will not consider the issue resolved. user_scenario.persona:
As a 41-year-old office administrator, you use your cellphone daily for both work and personal tasks. While you're familiar with common phone functions, you wouldn't call yourself a tech enthusiast.

Your technical skills are average - you handle standard smartphone features like calls, texts, email, and basic apps with ease. You understand the fundamental settings, but prefer clear, step-by-step guidance when trying something new.

In interactions, you're naturally friendly and patient. When receiving help, you listen attentively and aren't afraid to ask questions. You make sure to confirm your understanding and provide detailed feedback on each instruction you receive.

trajectory.content: Hi! How can I help you today?

Hi there — I’m actually the customer.

My mobile data has been really unreliable: it either gets extremely slow or stops working altogether. I’m currently abroad in France and I don’t have access to Wi‑Fi, so I really need to get this fixed and get excellent internet speed on my phone.

My name is John Smith and my number is 555-123-2002. What do you need me to check first?

I'm sorry to hear you're having trouble with your mobile data while abroad. I'll help you troubleshoot this issue.

First, let me look up your account using your phone number so I can check your line details.

{"customer_id": "C1001", "full_name": "John Smith", "date_of_birth": "1985-06-15", "email": "john.smith@example.com", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "address": {"street": "123 Main St", "city": "Anytown", "state": "CA", "zip_code": "90210"}, "account_status": "Active", "payment_methods": [{"method_type": "Credit Card", "account_number_last_4": "1235", "expiration_date": "12/2026"}], "line_ids": ["L1001", "L1002", "L1003"], "bill_ids": ["B1001", "B1002", "B1003"], "created_at": "2025-01-15 10:30:00", "last_extension_date": null, "goodwill_credit_used_this_year": 25.0}
I found your account, John. Now let me check the details of the line associated with your phone number to see its current status and roaming settings.

{"line_id": "L1001", "phone_number": "555-123-2001", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1001", "device_id": "D1001", "data_used_gb": 3.2, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": false, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2025-01-10", "last_sim_replacement_date": null, "suspension_start_date": null}

{"line_id": "L1002", "phone_number": "555-123-2002", "status": "Active", "plan_id": "P1002", "device_id": "D1002", "data_used_gb": 15.1, "data_refueling_gb": 0.0, "roaming_enabled": true, "contract_end_date": "2026-12-31", "last_plan_change_date": "2024-12-15", "last_sim_replacement_date": "2025-01-20", "suspension_start_date": null}

I found your line (555-123-2002). Good news – your line is **Active** and **roaming is already enabled** on your account, so you should be able to use data abroad in France.

Now let's diagnose what's happening on your device. Since you're experiencing slow or unreliable data, could you please do the following on your phone:

1. **First, check your status bar** - Look at the top of your screen and tell me what icons you see (signal bars, network type like 4G/5G, airplane mode icon, etc.)

2. **Run a speed test** - This will help us understand what kind of speeds you're getting right now.

Can you check those two things and let me know what you see?
Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%
Speed test failed: No Connection.

Sure — here’s what I’m seeing after checking:

1) **Status bar icons:** **Airplane Mode is ON** (airplane icon). I also see a **VPN connected** icon, and my **battery is 80%**.

2) **Speed test:** It **failed** and said **“No Connection.”**

Do you want me to turn **Airplane Mode off** first?

Yes, exactly! **Airplane Mode being ON** is why you have no connection – it disables all wireless radios, including cellular service.

Please go ahead and **turn Airplane Mode OFF**. Once you've done that, give it a few seconds for your phone to reconnect to the cellular network, and then:

1. **Check your status bar again** – let me know what icons you see (especially signal bars and network type like 4G/5G)
2. **Run another speed test** – and tell me the result

This should restore your connection, but we may still need to address the VPN and potentially other settings depending on what we see after.

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%
Speed test failed: No Connection.

Done — I turned **Airplane Mode OFF** and waited a bit.

1) **Status bar now shows:** **signal bars (poor)**, **2G**, **Data Disabled**, **VPN Connected**, **80% battery**.

2) **Speed test:** still **failed** with **“No Connection.”**

So it looks like Airplane Mode was part of it, but I still don’t have working data. What should I toggle next — **mobile data ON**?

Yes, exactly right! Your mobile data is currently disabled, which is why the speed test still shows "No Connection."

Please **turn mobile data ON**. After that, check your status bar again and run another speed test.

However, I can already see a couple of other things we'll likely need to address:
- **2G network** – This is too slow for reliable data and won't give you excellent speeds
- **VPN connected** – This could also be affecting your speeds

But let's tackle one thing at a time. Go ahead and enable mobile data first, then let me know what you see!

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%
Speed test failed: No Connection.

Okay, I turned **mobile data ON**.

**Status bar now:** **Poor signal**, **2G**, **Data Enabled**, **Data Saver is ON**, **VPN Connected**, **80% battery**.

But the **speed test still fails** with **“No Connection.”**

I’m getting a little frustrated now — I really need this to just work with excellent speed. What do you want me to try next: **turn off the VPN**, **turn off Data Saver**, or do something about the **2G** connection?

I completely understand your frustration, John. Let's get this sorted out.

Since you're abroad in France and need to use data on a foreign network, the issue is likely that **Data Roaming is turned OFF** on your phone. Even though your line is roaming-enabled on our end, your phone also has a setting that controls whether it's allowed to use data while roaming.

Please **turn Data Roaming ON** on your phone (this is sometimes called "toggle roaming"). Then check your status bar and run the speed test again.

Once we get a connection established, we'll address the **2G**, **Data Saver**, and **VPN** issues to get you to excellent speed – but first we need to get you connected!

Data Roaming is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%
Speed test failed: No Connection.

I just turned **Data Roaming ON**.

**Status bar:** still **Poor signal**, **2G**, **Data Enabled**, **Data Saver ON**, **VPN Connected**, **80% battery**.

**Speed test:** still **fails** with **“No Connection.”**

So even with roaming on, I’m still not getting any connection at all. What do you want me to do next?

That's strange – you should have a connection at this point since your line is roaming-enabled and you've turned on data roaming on your device.

Let's check a couple more things. Could you please:

1. **Check your SIM card status** – This will tell us if your SIM is being recognized properly
2. **Check your APN settings** – These are the technical settings your phone uses to connect to the data network

Let me know what both of those show!
Your SIM card is active and working.

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)

Here are the results:

1) **SIM status:** It says my **SIM card is active and working**.

2) **APN settings:**  
- **Current APN name:** `internet`  
- **MMSC URL:** `http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc`

So it looks like the SIM and APN are okay. What should we try next to actually get a connection (and then get it up to excellent speed)?

Thank you for checking those – your SIM and APN settings look fine.

Since we've confirmed:
- Airplane Mode is OFF ✓
- Mobile Data is ON ✓
- Data Roaming is ON ✓
- SIM is active ✓
- APN settings are correct ✓

And you're still on **2G with no data connection**, let's try changing your **network mode preference**. Being stuck on 2G could be preventing a proper data connection, especially while roaming.

Please **change your network mode preference to "4G/5G preferred"** (sometimes shown as `4g_5g_preferred`). This will allow your phone to connect to faster, more reliable networks.

Let me know what happens after you make that change – check your status bar and run a speed test again!

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%
Speed test failed: No Connection.

I switched the network mode to **4G/5G preferred**.

**Status bar now:** **Excellent signal**, **5G**, **Data Enabled**, **Data Saver ON**, **VPN Connected**, **80% battery**.

But weirdly, the **speed test still fails** with **“No Connection.”**

So the phone is showing 5G with excellent signal, but I still can’t actually get online. Should I try **disconnecting the VPN** next?

Yes, let's try **disconnecting the VPN** next. 

It's possible the VPN is causing the issue – if the VPN server is unreachable or misconfigured, it could be blocking all your internet traffic even though you have a strong 5G signal.

Please **disconnect the VPN** and then run the speed test again. Let me know what you get!

VPN disconnected successfully.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔋 80%
Speed test failed: No Connection.

I disconnected the VPN.

**Status bar now:** **Excellent signal**, **5G**, **Data Enabled**, **Data Saver ON**, **80% battery** (no VPN icon anymore).

But the **speed test still fails** with **“No Connection.”**

At this point I’m honestly pretty annoyed — everything looks “on” and strong, but I still have zero internet. What’s the next step you want me to try?

I completely understand your frustration, John – you're right, everything looks like it should be working. Let's try **turning off Data Saver mode** next.

Data Saver mode restricts background data and can sometimes interfere with connectivity in unexpected ways. Please **turn Data Saver OFF** and then run the speed test again.

If this doesn't work, we'll try a device reboot, which can often resolve stubborn connection issues when all the settings appear correct.

Data Saver Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed test failed: No Connection.

I turned **Data Saver OFF**.

**Status bar:** **Excellent signal**, **5G**, **Data Enabled**, **80% battery**.

But the **speed test still fails** with **“No Connection.”**

So that didn’t do it either. Do you want me to go ahead and **reboot the phone** next?

Yes, please go ahead and **reboot your phone**. 

At this point all your settings look correct – excellent 5G signal, data enabled, roaming on, no VPN or Data Saver interfering. A reboot will refresh all the network connections and often resolves these stubborn issues where everything *looks* right but isn't working.

Please reboot and then run the speed test again. Let me know the results!

Restarting network services...
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

Airplane Mode: OFF
SIM Card Status: active
Cellular Connection: connected
Cellular Signal: excellent
Cellular Network Type: 5G
Mobile Data Enabled: Yes
Data Roaming Enabled: Yes
Wi-Fi Radio: OFF
Wi-Fi Connected: No
Speed test failed: No Connection.

I rebooted the phone.

After it came back up, I checked again:

- **Network status:** Airplane Mode **OFF**, SIM **active**, cellular **connected**, signal **excellent**, **5G**, Mobile Data **Yes**, Data Roaming **Yes**, Wi‑Fi **OFF / not connected**.
- **Speed test:** still **fails** with **“No Connection.”**

So even after a reboot, it’s still not actually getting internet. What else can we do from here?

I'm really sorry you're still experiencing this, John. We've now exhausted all the standard troubleshooting steps:

- ✓ Airplane Mode OFF
- ✓ Mobile Data ON
- ✓ Data Roaming ON (both on your phone and enabled on your line)
- ✓ SIM active and working
- ✓ APN settings correct
- ✓ Network mode set to 4G/5G preferred
- ✓ VPN disconnected
- ✓ Data Saver OFF
- ✓ Device rebooted

Everything on your device and account appears to be configured correctly, yet you still have no data connection despite showing excellent 5G signal.

At this point, this issue is beyond what I can resolve with the tools and troubleshooting steps available to me. I need to transfer you to a human agent who can investigate further – there may be a deeper network or provisioning issue that requires advanced support.

arguments.summary:
Customer John Smith (Customer ID: C1001, Line ID: L1002, Phone: 555-123-2002) is traveling abroad in France and cannot get mobile data to work despite all troubleshooting steps being completed successfully. 

Account status: Line is Active, roaming is enabled on the account.

Troubleshooting completed:
- Turned Airplane Mode OFF
- Enabled Mobile Data
- Enabled Data Roaming on device
- Verified SIM is active
- Verified APN settings are correct (APN: internet, MMSC URL set)
- Changed network mode to 4G/5G preferred (phone now shows excellent 5G signal)
- Disconnected VPN
- Turned off Data Saver
- Rebooted device

Current device status: Excellent signal, 5G, Mobile Data enabled, Data Roaming enabled, Wi-Fi off, SIM active. However, speed test still fails with "No Connection."

The customer needs excellent internet speed for their trip and is understandably frustrated. This appears to be a deeper network or provisioning issue that requires advanced technical support investigation.

YOU ARE BEING TRANSFERRED TO A HUMAN AGENT. PLEASE HOLD ON.
###TRANSFER###

Let’s do **Option 1**. Please **refuel the full 2.0 GB** (so **$4.00**). I **don’t want to change my plan**, just need excellent speed restored right away.

What do you need from me to authorize/payment—are you sending a payment request to my phone?

I'll refuel your line with 2 GB of data right now. The $4.00 charge will be added to your next bill, so there's no immediate payment needed—it will just appear as a line item on your upcoming billing statement.

Let me process that for you now:
{"message": "Successfully added 2.0 GB of data for line L1002 for $4.00", "new_data_refueling_gb": "2.0", "charge": "4.0"}

Your data connectivity should now be restored. However, since you're traveling in France and want **excellent speed**, let me also run a quick check on your phone's settings to make sure everything is optimized for the best performance.

Status Bar: ✈️ Airplane Mode | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%

In my status bar I’m seeing:

- **VPN is connected (🔒)**
- **Battery 80%**

What should I turn off first—airplane mode or the VPN?

Let's start with **Airplane Mode**—that's the main culprit! When Airplane Mode is ON, it disables all wireless connections including cellular, which means you have no mobile data connection at all right now.

Please **turn off Airplane Mode first**. You can do this by toggling it off in your phone's settings or quick settings panel.

Once Airplane Mode is off and your cellular connection is restored, we'll check your speed. If it's still not excellent, we'll address the VPN next, as VPNs can sometimes slow down your connection.

Let me know once you've turned off Airplane Mode and tell me what you see in your status bar!

Airplane Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📵 Data Disabled | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%

I turned **Airplane Mode off**.

Now my status bar shows: **signal with “Poor”**, **2G**, **Data Disabled** (📵), **VPN still connected**, and **battery 80%**.

What should I do next to get back to excellent speed?

Great progress! I can see several things we need to fix. Let's address them in order of importance:

**Issues to fix:**
1. **Mobile Data is Disabled** - We need to turn this on
2. **2G Network** - This is too slow; we need to switch to 4G/5G
3. **VPN Connected** - This can slow down your connection
4. **Poor Signal** - Hopefully this improves with better network settings

Let's start by **turning on Mobile Data** and **switching your network mode preference to 4G/5G**. Please do the following:

1. **Toggle Mobile Data ON** in your phone's settings
2. **Change your network mode preference to "4G/5G Preferred"** (this is usually found in Settings > Mobile Network > Preferred Network Type)

Once you've done both of those, let me know what your status bar shows. After that, we'll disconnect the VPN and run a speed test to see where we stand!

I can do that, but I can only change **one thing at a time** on my end here.

Do you want me to **toggle Mobile Data ON first**, or **change the network mode to 4G/5G Preferred first**?

Let's **toggle Mobile Data ON first**—without that enabled, you won't have any data connection regardless of the network mode.

Go ahead and turn Mobile Data on, then let me know what your status bar shows!

Mobile Data is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶¹ Poor | 2G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%

Mobile Data is ON now.

Status bar shows: **Poor signal**, **2G**, **Data Enabled**, **Data Saver is ON** (🔽), **VPN Connected**, battery **80%**.

What next?

Good, data is now enabled! Now let's **change your network mode preference to "4G/5G Preferred"**—2G is far too slow for any meaningful data usage.

Please change your network mode to **4G/5G Preferred** and let me know what your status bar shows after that!

Preferred Network Mode set to: 4g_5g_preferred
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔽 Data Saver | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%

Done—I switched it to **4G/5G preferred**.

Now the status bar shows: **Excellent signal**, **5G**, **Data Enabled**, **Data Saver ON**, **VPN Connected**, battery **80%**.

Should we turn off **Data Saver** or disconnect the **VPN** next?

Excellent—you're now on 5G with excellent signal! That's a big improvement.

Let's **turn off Data Saver first**. Data Saver restricts background data usage and can throttle your overall speeds. Disabling it should help improve your data performance.

Please toggle Data Saver OFF and let me know what your status bar shows!

Data Saver Mode is now OFF.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔒 VPN Connected | 🔋 80%

Okay, **Data Saver is OFF** now.

Status bar shows: **Excellent signal**, **5G**, **Data Enabled**, **VPN Connected**, battery **80%**.

What’s the next step to make sure the actual speed is excellent?

We're almost there! Now let's **run a speed test** to see what your actual data speed is. This will tell us if the VPN is slowing things down or if you're already at excellent speed.

Please run a speed test on your phone and let me know what result you get!
Speed test failed: No Connection.

I ran the speed test, but it **failed with “No Connection.”**

That’s frustrating—my status bar shows **5G, excellent signal, and data enabled**, so I expected it to work. What should we try next—disconnect the VPN?

Yes, let's **disconnect the VPN** next. Even though your status bar shows a strong 5G connection, the VPN could be causing the connection to fail—sometimes VPN servers can be unresponsive or have issues routing traffic, especially when you're roaming internationally.

Please disconnect the VPN and then run the speed test again. Let me know what results you get!

VPN disconnected successfully.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%
Speed test failed: No Connection.

I disconnected the VPN (it’s gone from the status bar now), and the status bar still shows **Excellent signal, 5G, Data Enabled**.

But the speed test **still fails with “No Connection.”**

At this point I’m getting a bit irritated—what else can we check that would actually get me back to **excellent** working data?

I completely understand your frustration—let's keep troubleshooting to get this resolved.

Since your status bar looks perfect but data still isn't working, there may be an issue with your APN settings. Let me check a couple of things on your phone:

Could you please **check your APN settings** and also **check your SIM status**? Let me know what both show—this will help us identify if there's a configuration issue blocking your connection.

I can check those, but I can only do **one check at a time**.

Do you want me to check **APN settings** first, or **SIM status** first?

Let's check **APN settings first**—incorrect APN settings are a very common cause of data connectivity issues even when everything else looks fine.

Please check your APN settings and let me know what you see!

Current APN Name: internet
MMSC URL (for picture messages): http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc
(These are technical settings, usually best left unchanged.)

I checked the APN settings. It shows:

- **Current APN name:** `internet`  
- **MMSC URL:** `http://mms.carrier.com/mms/wapenc`

What should I check next—SIM status, or should we reset the APN?

Those APN settings look correct (APN name is set and MMSC URL is present). Let's **check your SIM status next** to make sure the SIM card is working properly—a SIM issue could explain why you're showing a connection but can't actually use data.

Please check your SIM status and let me know what it shows!
Your SIM card is active and working.

SIM status shows the **SIM is active and working**.

So APN looks fine, SIM is fine, I’m on **5G with excellent signal**, and data is enabled… but I still get **“No Connection”** on the speed test.

What do we try next?

This is puzzling—everything on your phone looks correct. Since your device settings all appear fine, let me check if there's an issue on the account side. 

Even though roaming is enabled on your account, let me verify there isn't something else blocking your data connection. I'm going to try toggling the roaming setting on your phone to refresh the connection.

Please **toggle Data Roaming OFF and then back ON** on your phone. Since you're in France, your phone needs roaming enabled to connect to the local networks there.

First, please **toggle roaming OFF**, then let me know, and we'll turn it back on.

Data Roaming is now ON.
Status Bar: 📶⁴ Excellent | 5G | 📱 Data Enabled | 🔋 80%

I tried to toggle Data Roaming **OFF**, but the result I got says **Data Roaming is now ON** (status bar still: **Excellent / 5G / Data Enabled / 80%**).

Do you want me to toggle it again to try to turn it **OFF**?

That tells us Data Roaming was already OFF before, and toggling it just turned it ON—which is actually what we need! Since you're traveling in France, Data Roaming must be ON for your phone to use data on foreign networks.

Now that Data Roaming is ON, please **run the speed test again** and let me know what result you get!
Speed Test Result: 275.00 Mbps (Excellent). Connection is very fast.

I ran the speed test again and now it works: **275.00 Mbps (Excellent)**.

So it looks like the main fix was turning **Data Roaming ON** (and earlier we also turned off airplane mode, enabled data, switched to 4G/5G, and turned off data saver / VPN).

As long as it stays at **excellent**, I’m good now. ###STOP###

user 또는 tool에 없는 정보·절차·주관적 추천을 만들지 않는다

data saver를 끄도록 한다

amount를 묻고 plan 단가로 계산한 가격을 확인받는다

mobile data/MMS 절차 전에 cellular service를 복구한다

task_context.goal_text:
The user is experiencing issues with their mobile data. They are unable to use their phone to browse the internet, and the status bar shows 'No Service'. Customer name: John Smith, phone number: 555-123-2002, current location: abroad in France. They will consider the issue resolved when speed test returns excellent internet speed. They will not change their mobile data plan but they will refuel 2.0 GB of data if necessary.

You mobile data is not working properly. It either stops working or is very slow. You want to fix it and absolutely want to get excellent internet speed on your phone. You are not willing to accept any other internet speed (poor, fair or good). You do not have access to wifi.

If the agent suggests actions that don't immediately fix the issue, follow their guidance but express mild frustration after the first unsuccessful attempt. You will consider the issue resolved only when speed test returns excellent internet speed and nothing else. If it returns poor, fair or good, you will not consider the issue resolved. You are willing to refuel 2.0 GB of data if necessary, but you do not want to change your mobile data plan. If the tool call does not return updated status information, you might need to perform another tool call to get the updated status. 
Whenever the agent asks you about your device, always ground your responses on the results of tool calls. 
For example: If the agent asks what the status bar shows, always ground your response on the results of the `get_status_bar` tool call. If the agent asks if you are able to send an MMS message, always ground your response on the results of the `can_send_mms` tool call.
Never make up the results of tool calls, always ground your responses on the results of tool calls.
If you are unsure about whether an action is necessary, always ask the agent for clarification.
